💡 Tip: Don't try to see everything. Move fast between the main wings to maximize your time. If you don’t want to buy a ticket, touring outside around the Cour Napoléon to see the historic palace architecture is a highly valuable, free alternative.

This was a must on our list of activities for our first trip to Paris and it didn't disappoint. There's so much here! We did the Mona Lisa and the Venus de Milo but enjoyed all the other galleries as well.

We were lucky enough to be there for the Martin Schongauer exhibition, which was amazing. It was also included in the cost of admission.

The shop is huge! And in two halves, so don't forget to allow enough time for this as well.

We had a drink in the Le Cafe Mollien. The outside terrace was surprising cool given the temperature was about 30° and has amazing views over the Pyramid (see the photo).

We went during heat wave of May 2026 and didn't fancy the long wait outside in the sun. Don't forget that the pyramid is not the only entrance - we went to the Carrousel entrance instead. We still had to queue but it was much shorter than the queue outside and it was underground with air conditioning. I'd also recommend turning up early for your slot - we arrived (mostly) on time and the queue was already long. It was a Bank Holiday so peak time!

Not for people who cannot walk 40k steps a day!
An incredible museum with breathtaking art and history around every corner. The Mona Lisa was amazing to see in person, but the entire museum is filled with masterpieces. The architecture of the Louvre itself is stunning, especially the glass pyramid. Definitely worth visiting if you’re in Paris — just wear comfortable shoes because there’s so much to explore!

Stunning museum in the former royal palace. Very busy but an incredible collection. The apartments from Napoleon III were very impressive. You never have enough time to see it all. Best to decide upfront what you want to see and focus.

Toooo tooo huge to cover in a day. It is the biggest museum ever. But yes must visit place and they have kept and preserved everything so well. Buy tickets in advance the queue outside was very very huge. We had advanced timed tickets so we were saved.

very cool, love the contrast between old and new architecture, the old buildings look so grand and intricate, while the newer pyramid looks modern and shiny. i love everything about it, area is fun with a big shopping mall underground, nearby gardens are beautiful and peaceful to stroll though, lovely place to visit, i really enjoyed it :)

Try getting there early in the morning. Pay attention of which line you are standing in. Some are for people with pre paid tickets. You can book in advance but you can also go without one and buy your ticket there. For students under 26, admission is free.
You should at least dedicate 4 hours ( I still feel like it’s not enough if you want to spend time on different pieces)

Easily one of the best museums in the world and a must-see, but you need a strategy! First, skip the food and coffee inside—it's overpriced, mediocre, and there’s nowhere to sit. Second, avoid the ground floor bathrooms unless you want to wait 20 minutes in line. Most importantly, be clever and ignore the "Start Here" sign, otherwise you'll walk for hours and be too exhausted to enjoy the best parts. Grab a map and go straight to the Denon wing to see the highlights (like the Mona Lisa) first, then explore the rest at your own pace.

The Louvre Museum is recognized as part of a UNESCO World Heritage site since 1991. This heritage area extends along the Seine river from the Louvre to the Eiffel Tower and includes other iconic monuments like Notre-Dame Cathedral and the Place de la Concorde. The museum typically draws millions of visitors annually, making it one of the most-visited museums in the world. The Louvre is the world's largest art museum and a historic monument, originally built as a fortress in the 12th century. The museum houses famous masterpieces such as the Mona Lisa of Leonardo Da Vinci and the Venus de Milo.
The palace complex is situated along the Right Bank of the Seine River, adjacent to the Tuileries Garden. Underneath the pyramid is a large lobby that provides access to the museum's various wings, along with ticketing and information desks. The museum, which was originally built as a fortress in the 12th century and later served as a royal palace, has been open to the public since 1793.

The Louvre Museum is an absolute masterpiece and a total bucket-list experience when you are in Paris! As the world's largest art museum, it is housed in a stunning historic palace that blends classic architecture with the iconic modern glass pyramid out front. Inside, it is a massive treasure trove where you can see legendary pieces like the Mona Lisa and the Venus de Milo up close. It is incredibly vast, so it is best to pick a few wings you are most excited about rather than trying to see it all in one day.

Incredible!  Highly recommend getting a private tour guide to take you through. Affordable and they can tell you more about the museum and the works than you can imagine.  Also skip long entrance line. Incredible experience!  Oh make sure you see the Moat.

We booked tickets online for 9am, when it opened. The queue was very long and we got in at about 9:30. However, it wasn’t too busy inside for the first hour or two so most areas felt quiet. It did get very busy around midday with most areas feeling crowded.
Quite a few areas were closed for refurbishment when we went, but we still thoroughly enjoyed it. It’s a fantastic building with lots to see.

Huge museum with a lot to see. I like the part about Italian and French painting and also the part about the Ancient Greece and ancient Egypt. But it was a kind of disappointing since it did not have a lot of things related to the Hellenistic period, which I personally am most interested in.Many of the paintings are worldly well-known, and those paintings appeared in my elementary school art textbook. The overall exhibitions were very nice, but the toilet was not that clean. Make sure to leave at least 3 hours for this place.

We arrived early for our 9.30am entrance time, and we were haopy that we did, before we knew it, the queue was huge and wrapping around one of the sides. We really enjoyed our experience, headed straight for the Mona Lisa because we expected it to become very very busy as time went on. We didnt have to wait long to get to the front where we were able to take photos. I recommend the tour with head phones, very informative of the paintings as you go through the rooms. We were in The Louve for about 3 hours. There is a lot to see.

Wow!

Amazing.

The largest and most important museum in the world, yet it manages to be relaxing and spacious and not feel crowded by having one art piece next to another! It's a pleasure to visit and a one week ticket would be a great idea for art lovers 👌😁

You simply can't grasp the size of this thing as you simply think you had seen it all you stumble yet into another wing 🙄

And the building is by itself a masterpiece!

I love it! 6 stars out of 5!

Was on a "Trafalgar Tours Experience of France Tour" last week and on last Friday it was our day to get a glimpse of the most famous, iconic and biggest Art museum in the world. In looking up the statistics, and with the crowds it would take more than two weeks of about 4 hours a day to absorb most of what this museum has under it's roofs (plural). It's truly staggering and really big !! I was pleasantly surprised to be able to get close enough to the"Mona Lisa" to get really good pix 📸. Hope to one day return and visit more than the less than one percent we saw this day. I'm told there is another portrait of a model similar to the "Mona Lisa" and our expert Louvre Licensed guide said that hardly anyone hours to see it. I believe it was done by a student of Leonardo Da Vinci and is bigger than "Mona Lisa".

The staff there were great and it's truly amazing how they handle or corral all of it Visitors and guests like us without much wait time.

Our group got to see the room and window that the robbery of the crown 👑 Jewels started and where they exited out of. I hope someone comes forward and that the Lovure gets these priceless artifacts back. Surely someone or some private collector knows something.

I can't wait to come back one day and continue to explore this truly beautiful and huge huge huge museum.

Was all that I expected, wonderful place to visit if you like or study art history. Recommend you buy tickets in advance. I was lucky with the walk in line that only took 1 hour waiting during the slow touris season mid March. Too much too see, this place is huge.  $36e for non residents, would I do it again, No. Everything you see here are in history books. Just being here to know that you were here seem to be the trend.

No place on Earth can be compared to the Louvre, what an experience! However we were very disappointed to learn that unfortunately a lot of attractions were closed when we went including room 700 (where most famous paintings are located), Napoleon’s apartment, and of course the jewelry room due to recent events. Before you purchase tickets make sure to check if everything you want to see will be available on that date. Ensure to also get those tickets ahead of time and get there at least 30 minutes earlier than booked times. Security lines can be crazy!

The Louvre at Night, a Walk That Needs No Tickets
I didn’t go inside the museum. This visit was something else, a nighttime walk through Paris that ended in front of the Louvre when there was barely anyone left.
Seeing it at night is a different experience from the typical daytime postcard. The pyramid lights up from within and reflects on the water of the fountains, and the palace buildings are bathed in a warm light that gives them an almost theatrical feel. There’s something quiet and elegant about the scene, something that during the day, with so many tourists around, you simply don’t get to notice.
I really recommend it as a way to close a night in Paris. There’s no need to plan it, just let your walk end there. If you’re coming from the Seine or from the Tuileries Gardens, the route practically takes you on its own, and arriving at the Louvre courtyard has that final touch every good night seems to ask for.
On the practical side, the area is safe to get to, to stay a while, and to head back from. Public transport is very convenient, there are several metro lines nearby, the most direct one being the Palais Royal–Musée du Louvre station, and city buses also run through the area until late. Getting back to your hotel from there is simple, no matter which neighborhood you’re staying in.
In short: A must-see and completely free, an experience you can enjoy without lines, without rushing, and with a view that’s hard to forget. If you’re putting together your itinerary, leave it for the end of the day. It’s the best way to close a night in Paris.
Very nice experience. I think is a must to visit if at Paris. Remember to buy tickets online. Spent around 3 hours at here. Very big museum and definitely worth to come.

Visiting Louvre Museum during my first trip to Paris was honestly an amazing experience and something I highly recommend to anyone visiting the city.

I’m usually not the type of person who goes out of their way to visit museums, but the Louvre felt like an absolute must. Being surrounded by so much art, history, and incredible architecture was such a surreal experience.

It was really cool seeing famous pieces in person — artworks you’ve heard about in school, seen in movies, magazines, or online for years. Seeing them right in front of you gives a completely different appreciation for them.

The museum itself is massive, and honestly I wish I had more time to explore all of it because there’s just so much to see. Everywhere you turn, there’s another beautiful room, sculpture, or painting.

I’ll definitely be returning to Paris again someday, and visiting the Louvre will absolutely be on my must-do list again. Absolutely stunning.

Visiting Paris and not going to see the louvre would be such a shame. This is the biggest museum in the world and you'll quickly realise it when inside, there's so many amazing things to see that you would need a full week. One of the most hidden gems is Napoleon's Apartments, get a map and find it! Been here at least 10 times and everytime i find something new and equally interesting.
Life hack: buy the tickets online and do not stand in line near the pyramid, there are 2 other entrances, one on the shopping mall beneath and one on the street.

A wonderful museum. This was my 4th visit to Paris & finally got to see the Mona Lisa! We hadn’t planned on a visit but as it was scorching hot, there was no queue outside. Will definitely return to see more of the art. Lady Liberty doesn’t have the same crowns but is a beautiful painting.

Tickets only available online so get them before you visit. They do half hour slots so once at the glass pyramid entrance look for your time slot queue and join it. On e inside grab a map and good luck, the gallery is HUGE. Bit confusing to navigate but we saw what we needed to, Mona Lisa, Venus de Milo etc. Once inside go straight to the Mona Lisa as its like a bun fight in there. Very beautiful gallery with lots to see. You really need a good few hours if you want to see all of it.

The fact that entry is free for EU students was amazing! If you want to see everything, I highly recommend dedicating at least 6 hours to this place—it is massive.
Even though it’s giant, navigating your way around was surprisingly easy just by looking at the map, and honestly, it was pretty fun exploring the different rooms. Everything inside is incredibly grand and breathtaking

Mit Reservierung über eine Stunde anstehen. Wirre Besucherführung. Kein Audio-Guide (war bekannt, aber auch kein Ersatz). Seltsame Zusammenstellung aus unterschiedlichsten Epochen. Alles irgendwie konzeptlos. Von Museumspädagogik hat man anscheinend noch nie gehört. Ja und dann gibt es da halbes Dutzend must-sees vor denen sich hundert Menschen drängeln, oder Instagrammer posieren. Schrecklich. Ich war zum vierten Mal in meinen Leben im Louvre, das letzte Mal allerdings vor 30 Jahren. Und ich gehe bestimmt nicht wieder hin. Es ist mittlerweile eines der am meisten überbewerteten Museen.

The world knows the Louvre - and for good reason.  It's simply one of the greatest collections of artifacts ever brought together.  Every item has a story.  It would take days to go through it all, so I'd sincerely suggest a guide to maximize your time viewing the impressive collection.  If you can, plan your visit before or after summer, and avoid holidays.  I'd also suggest viewing the more iconic items close to closing time.  The crowds thin out and you'll have a better viewing experience.  Otherwise, try hard to be in the first group to enter and quickly go to the more popular items as quickly as possible.  Either way, have a plan.  Visit the museum's website and know what you want to see and where you need to go.  Absolutely buy tickets ahead of time.  There's stairs, escalators, lifts, and ramps to transit.  We walked 3 miles in just over 2 hours, so expect to walk - a lot.

What can I say standing in front of these historic and amazing art pieces... speechless. We booked ticket one day before and didn't wait too long for queueing because we arrived 20min before the visit time(you can enter earlier). There was many many people in front of The Mona Lisa, but if you're by yourself, you can quickly squeeze in and take a photo at the frontline expeditiously. The flow of people was quite ok, not overly crowded and some places have better ventilation with cool air conditioning. There were chairs anywhere so you can rest anytime you need. Remember to take a look at the ceiling everywhere you go cuz there are loads of detailed arts and designs there too!!

Of course, when you are in Paris you have to visit the Louvre. We booked the earliest ticket because we wanted to get away from the crowds.

When we got there, I think we entered from the back of the museum and therefore the line was not long at all, also maybe because of the time. But when we got in we saw the long lines from the main entrance.

The goal was to see Mona Lisa first, then we can move on to other exhibits. The directions were very clear as probably a lot of people are here to see the painting. We managed to see her up close, which was nice to finally see something you hear about so often up close.

We then move on to other exhibits. We did not look at all the exhibits but only chose the more popular ones like Venus de Milo, the Winged Victory of Samothrace, Liberty Leading the People etc.

It was an enjoyable visit. Make sure to get the tickets early before going!

The Lourve museum was fantastic! Aside from the famous Mona Lisa, there is a wide variety of sections you can visit. We pre-ordered the tickets online for early morning and still had to wait about 30 mins to get in, but the wait was worth it. The staff were helpful and easily accessible on each floor. We spent about 2.5 hours here. Wear comfy shoes, there will be a lot of walking.

The Louvre is the best attraction in Paris hands down. Don’t let anybody tell you it’s a tourist trap. Yes, there will be a lot of people and it’s huge so I recommend a tour that will also skip the really long lines at general entry. I also went during a very busy Sunday so there’s that.

I absolutely adored the red French rooms. The Wedding of Napoleon painting was one of my favourites. The Wedding Feast at Cana is also wonderful to look at, right across the painting of Mona Lisa.

From the stunning architecture to the incredible art like the Mona Lisa and Venus de Milo, every moment felt magical. The museum is huge and full of history—I could’ve stayed forever. A must-visit if you're in Paris!

I absolutely loved the Louvre. It’s an incredible museum and honestly hard not to be amazed by what’s inside.

I’d strongly recommend using the free maps they give at the entrance. The place is massive, and without a map it’s very easy to miss important sections.

Of course, there are world famous masterpieces like the Mona Lisa, Liberty Leading the People, the Venus de Milo, and the Winged Victory of Samothrace but beyond those, the museum is full of countless stunning works.

We bought our tickets in advance and waited no more than 5 minutes to get in. Meanwhile, the line for people without tickets was extremely long.

Overall, an unforgettable experience, but definitely plan ahead and don’t skip the map.
The best museum I’ve ever visited! Everything was amazing. You can navigate through the exhibits to learn about history and experience the sensory environment of that era.

Be prepared for the queue if you don’t buy a ticket in advance. We used the secret door and waited only to pass through security. Our reservation time was normally scheduled for 10:30am but due to jet lag we made it there around 1:30pm. They still honored the reservation.

We prepared for a lot of people! It was very hot. I had to hydrate as I felt faint.

If you want to experience the entire museum, you will need a full day at the least. We went for 2 hours and only explored 2/3 of one wing. It can be exhausting but it’s worth it.

They sell snacks and beverages (hot & cold).

Not much room to sit and dine due to so many people.

Be prepared to see people sleeping in corners and large tour groups. If you have the time, take your time. Enjoy!

Visiting the Louvre Museum was one of the highlights of our 10 day trip to Paris in April 2025. We decided to go on our very first day in the city, and it immediately set the tone for what ended up being a trip of a lifetime.

The scale of the Louvre is hard to describe until you see it in person. The building itself is incredible, and once inside you quickly realize you could spend days there and still not see everything. We walked for hours exploring gallery after gallery and constantly found ourselves stopping in amazement at the paintings, sculptures, and artifacts we had only ever heard about or seen in books.

One of the most memorable moments was finally seeing the Mona Lisa in person. Like many people say, it is smaller than you might expect, but it is still incredible to see such a famous painting up close. We also loved seeing the Winged Victory in person, which is absolutely breathtaking when you stand in front of it.

Another highlight for us was seeing the French crown jewels. It was fascinating to see such historic pieces in real life, especially things you normally only see in documentaries or history books.

Be prepared to do a lot of walking because the museum is enormous, but everywhere you look there is something amazing. Even just walking through the halls and rooms of the palace itself is an experience.

The line to get in took about an hour when we visited, but it was completely worth the wait. Once inside, the experience more than makes up for it.

If you are visiting Paris, the Louvre is absolutely a must see. It was an unforgettable day for us and one of the most memorable parts of our entire trip. We cannot wait to come back one day and explore even more of it.

A treasure chest of humanity's greatest creations.  Too many people, too many stairsteps. Pleasantly surprised at the plentiful snacks and restrooms. The map placards are practically useless for navigating, even if you are "good at maps".  Not enough signage to direct a self-tour, but at least they get you to the Mona Lisa effectively.   Go early and you'll have less waiting in lines.  A remarkable place in this world.

The place is huge and filled with amazing pieces. We booked a tour, which was a good choice, since we entered without queuing and was guided to the most famous pieces. Crowded with people, but less than what we expected (Februar 2026, afternoon)

Skip-the-line tickets are a must! Make sure you plan ahead and buy your ticket online before you come.

I was already in awe from the very first step inside the Louvre Museum. The place is incredibly grand, historical, and huge. Every room feels impressive, and there is just so much to see. Honestly, I don’t think it’s possible to finish the whole museum in one day.

It is truly a must-visit place when you are in Paris.

About the Mona Lisa — it might feel a bit overrated because everyone wants to see it (and yes, I was also one of them 😄). It’s very crowded and the painting itself is not very big. But still, it was worth seeing at least once, even if it was only from the back of the crowd.

A must see while in Paris. Wear comfortable shoes. It gets pretty crowded, but there are so many wonderful exhibits you barely notice the crowds. Go early and head straight to the Monna Lisa (correct spelling). After you capture that famous photo you feel less pressure and can see the rest of the museum. Note: it is massive!
The Lourve is attached to a mall and train station all without going back outside.

Never got lost, but there is so much to see. Toilets were either packed or out of order, so be prepared to look for one that works. It is so huge but very clean in the viewing locations where the works of Art are.

The Louvre is a vast and incredibly comprehensive museum — it’s simply impossible to see everything in a single visit. It’s definitely a good idea to buy your tickets in advance and plan which sections you want to explore before you go.

We spent about three hours there and were only able to cover the Ancient Egypt, Ancient Greece, and Italian paintings sections. The room where the Italian masterpieces are displayed — including the Mona Lisa — is extremely crowded, making it quite difficult to enter and get close to the painting.

That said, the museum is absolutely worth visiting, and it truly deserves multiple visits to fully appreciate what it has to offer.

One of the most beautiful and valuable places everyone should visit at least once in their lifetime. The collection of art and historical artifacts in the Louvre is beyond imagination. The museum is very well-organized, though it gets incredibly crowded (especially around masterpieces like the Mona Lisa). Make sure to book your tickets in advance. Do not miss this extraordinary attraction while in Paris!

Certo è inevitabile, biglietto o no fare quasi un'ora di fila vista la folla oceanica, ma una volta dentro le dimensioni immense di questo luogo ti lasciano viaggiare tra le meraviglie presenti quasi in solitaria, assorto tra i pensieri e le emozioni che ti creano.
Il luogo è già una costruzione meravigliosa in se, ma passeggiare nei suoi sottrrranei una volta canali inondati che lo circondavano, e poi incontrare pezzi estimabili e magnifici della storia egizia, sumera, babilonese passando poi per gli assiri su su fino ai romani e poi le opere d'arte della pittura e le sculture bellissime quasi contemporanee ti lascia senza fiato.
Da visitare assolutamente!

Spesso I musei più noti possono rivelarsi deludenti. Non è il caso del Louvre. Ho visitato il museo con una guida per un tour di due ore, concentrata soprattutto sulle più conosciute opere di pittori italiani e francesi. Esperienza stupenda. Non tanto la Gioconda che è difficile da apprezzare visto l'enorme massa di turisti che vi si accalcano contro,  quanto piuttosto le Nozze di Cana, La vergine delle rocce, san Giovanni Battispa (forse peraltro il quadro più enigmatico di Leonardo, ancora più della Gioconda) e tanti altri. Dopo il tour guidato mi sono fermato altre due ore all'interno del museo per apprezzare le sale dedicate alle opere scultoree greche e romane, i manufatti dell'antico Egitto e gli arredi del Palazzo Reale. Ci si potrebbero passare davvero giornate interessanti! Ottima esperienza.

Sehr bewundernswerte Austellung, wahrscheinlich die größte Austellung die ich je sehen werde... Den Preis musste ich nicht bezahlen da es freier Museum-Tag war, aber ich empfehle es auch so. Es gibt massenweise viele Bilder und Skulpturen aus verschiedenen Ländern wie Griechenland, Ägypten, Frankreich, Italien, etc. Was mich faszinierte war wie GROẞ das Innere des Louvres war! Hätte ich mir nicht vorstellen können. Und noch dazu war das Personal sehr nett. Ich liebe es so ein Ort zu besuchen wo auch Filme wie "Lupin" gefmt wurden. Sehr empfehlenswert.
Einziger Nachteil: Die Warteschlange.

Même si l'on ne peut savoir combien de siècles nous contemplent du haut de la pyramide du Louvre, les visiteurs d'en bas venant du monde entier sont toujours dans l'émerveillement. L'entretien des lieux et en particulier des bâtiments est toujours remarquable et l'immensité de l'esplanade dilue suffisamment la foule pour apprécier l'endroit chargé d'histoire. Cette fois-ci nous resterons qu'à l'extérieur faute de temps.

Tickets hadden wij van te voren besteld. Echt aan te raden anders sta je heel lang in de rij. Per tijdslot staat er een rij waar je moet verzamelen, dat is boven bij de piramide.
Wij waren binnen 7 minuten binnen. Alles was goed geregeld en de wc’s waren schoon.

Mona Lisa hebben we gezien. Ze is kleiner dan verwacht. Erg druk maar het wachten waard.

Het Louvre is zo groot dat wij nog een keer terug moeten komen. Wij vonden het een geweldige ervaring en ook voor onze kinderen van 11 en 8.

Impresionante por fuera y por dentro!! Mucha cultura, diferentes imperios, diferentes países y muchas etapas de la historia mundial.
Me gustó mucho conocer este museo, es muy grande y no terminamos de explorarlo.
La audio guía ayuda bastante aunque si puedes contratar un tour sería mejor para ir a los lugares más importantes.

Esperienza unica.
Immenso, affascinante ma anche a tratti dispersivo; merita sicuramente la giornata spesa tra le sale ed i corridoi di questo palazzo bellissimo.
Consiglio sicuramente di prenotare i biglietti in anticipo ed anche di presentarsi una buona mezz'ora prima rispetto all'orario scelto per mettersi in coda.

El Louvre es imponente en cada rincón. Aunque ver sus colecciones es un viaje único en el tiempo, caminar por sus patios exteriores es un placer igual de grande. Disfrutar de la majestuosidad de sus fachadas, ver cómo cambia la luz sobre la piedra y pasear rodeada de tanta historia es una experiencia mágica. Para mí, el museo se disfruta tanto adentro como afuera. Absolutamente imprescindible!
Le Louvre est un endroit magique. Si ses œuvres d'art sont exceptionnelles, j'aime tout particulièrement me promener dans ses cours historiques. Marcher au milieu de cette architecture grandiose, admirer la pyramide et ressentir l'histoire à chaque pas est un pur bonheur. C'est un lieu magnifique où l'on se sent privilégié à chaque visite.

広大な館内に、世界的に有名な作品が数多く展示されている美術館。
どこを見ても見どころがあり、歩いているだけでも圧倒される空間です。

ガラスのピラミッドや開放感のある中庭も印象的で、建物そのものも楽しめます。
彫刻や絵画、工芸品まで幅広く展示されていて、ゆっくり見て回るとかなり時間が必要です。

とにかく広いため、見たい作品とその場所を事前に調べておくことが大切です。
あらかじめ決めておかないと、目当てのものを見逃してしまうこともあります。

ハンムラビ法典を見たくて向かったのですが、急にエリアが閉鎖されてしまい、係の方に外へ出るよう案内されました。
なんとか見ることはできましたが、閉館まではまだ時間があったため、理由が分からず少し戸惑いました。

音声ガイドを利用すると理解が深まり、より楽しめました。
天井や装飾も美しく、作品だけでなく空間全体が見どころになっています。

一度では回りきれないほどの規模で、何度でも訪れたくなる場所です。

Dünyanın en büyük sanat müzesi olan Louvre Müzesi, eskiden bir kraliyet sarayı olarak kullanılıyormuş. İçeride o kadar çok eser var ve müze o kadar büyük ki, tek bir günde her şeyi görmek neredeyse imkansız. Eğer sanata ilginiz varsa buraya mutlaka tam bir gününüzü ayırın derim.
Biletleri kesinlikle önceden online almanızı öneririm. Piramit tarafından girerseniz saat tabelalarının olduğu kuyruklar var; hangi saate bilet aldıysanız o sıraya giriyorsunuz. Çok kalabalık ve çok sıra oluyor. İçeride ücretsiz vestiyer var ama çabuk dolabiliyor, yer bulursanız eşyalarınızı bırakıp rahat gezmek büyük avantaj.
Kat planları biraz karışık Gitmeden önce görmek istediğiniz bölümleri belirlemek çok önemli. Önce hedeflediğiniz yerleri gezin, çünkü bir noktadan sonra yorulmaya başlıyorsunuz. Biz önce Mona Lisa’ya gittik; kalabalığı takip edince yerini direkt bulabiliyorsunuz zaten. Burada tam bir kaos oluyor, sakince tabloya bakamıyorsunuz. Önünde fotoğraf çekmek için yeterli alan yok. Tablolar, heykeller ve mobilyalar gerçekten büyüleyici. Sadece çok fazla insan olduğu için gezerken gürültülü oluyor.

Стояли в очереди час, потом решил сходить узнать обстановку, выяснил что музей с утра не работал, и заработает ли не известно, т.к. какая то забастовка в Париже . Никаких табличек ничего не было, люди просто приходили и вставали в очередь, и думали что очередь уменьшается, потому что те кто доходят до входа узнают все это и уходят. В общем дичь полнейшая. В итоге ушли и вернулись позже, в музей попали в 3 часа дня только. Что касается экспозиции то надо закладывать на все хотя бы целый день.

Лувр — сердце мировой культуры и символ Парижа
Лувр поражает масштабом и историей. Это бывший королевский дворец, превращённый в крупнейший музей мира, где собрано более 35 000 произведений искусства — от древнеегипетских артефактов до шедевров эпохи Возрождения. Само здание — произведение архитектуры: сочетание классических фасадов и современной стеклянной пирамиды архитектора И. М. Пея создаёт уникальный контраст старого и нового Парижа.
Главные сокровища музея — «Мона Лиза» Леонардо да Винчи, «Венера Милосская», «Ника Самофракийская», «Свобода, ведущая народ» Делакруа. Но не стоит ограничиваться только ими: коллекции античного, исламского, египетского и французского искусства заслуживают отдельного внимания.
Совет: приходите утром, чтобы избежать очередей, и заранее выберите, что хотите увидеть — музей огромен, и за один день всё не охватить. Для удобства можно взять аудиогид или экскурсию: гиды рассказывают интересные детали, которые оживляют историю каждого шедевра.
Инфраструктура отличная — кафе, сувенирные лавки, зоны отдыха. Персонал вежливый, навигация понятная. Вечером, когда пирамиду подсвечивают, Лувр превращается в настоящий символ Парижа.
Лувр — место, где искусство говорит на всех языках. Даже короткий визит оставляет глубокое впечатление и желание вернуться снова.

Por alguna razón la web no me dejó comprar los tickets así que tuve que ir y hacer fila para entrar, la realidad es que no fueron más de 10 minutos para estar en la pirámide y luego el self-service, muy rápido todo, supongo que en verano la cosa es diferente y si conviene más comprar los boletos desde antes.

Como tip, marquen su ruta y piensen que es lo que quieren ver, en mi experiencia he estado 3 veces y no termino de recorrer el museo, esta vez me salté algunas salas para ir a mis preferidos y mostrárselos a mis acompañantes. También tomen un mapa del counter y pregunten a los guardias de las salas, ellos les ayudarán a salir de la forma más eficiente, entre la gente, el caos, las escaleras es fácil perderse o salir a mismo lugar. También recomiendo dar un vistazo a la página oficial, ya que ahorita mismo la sala de arte egipcio y Oriente Medio está cerrada, igual que el departamento de Napoleón.

Obwohl ich ein Ticket für 9:30 gebucht hatte, wurde ich auf 11h vertröstet, ein verspäteter Einlass, keiner wußte warum. Um 11h dann eine ca 500m lange Warteschlange...Geduld war erforderlich!
Unabhängig davon ist das Museum gewaltig beeindruckend und die vielen Besucher verlaufen sich!

Pieno di arte, peccato la mia visita è stata penalizzata perche avevo una prenotazione dopo e sono stata poco tempo dentro. Ma è veramente magnifico.
Prenotare non è cosi facile, puoi quando arrivi vedrai le file con gli orari dove dovrai aspettare il tuo turno per entrare. Passati i controlli dalla piramide cerchi a quale salone andare.

È senza dubbio una tappa obbligatoria per chi si reca a Parigi. Ma ritengo che un museo così importante debba avere un tour ben organizzato perché c'è il rischio serissimo, anzi certo, che in una giornata intera si vede forse solo la metà del museo, ed inoltre se si sbaglia giornata la fila per entrare porta all'esaurimento nervoso. Detto questo, in questo luogo vi sono tesori dal valore inestimabile.

Há muito mais que Monalisa no museu. Isso de horário é balela, porque você entra numa fila de, no mínimo, 40 minutos de espera. E o museu é gigantesco, impossível ver tudo em apenas um dia. Mas as obras são fantásticas, maior que o British Museum e Deutsches Historiches Museum. Recomendo em reservar um dia inteiro para ele.

Un lugar indiscutible para visitar, es único, maravilloso, majestuoso. Uno de los museos más icónicos del mundo. Tienes que ir con tiempo y paciencia para entrar por que la cola es eterna. El lugar está muy cuidado, los jardines son preciosos.
Para mi la mejor época para visitar París es entre abril y mayo. Así puedes realizar la espera para entrar sin calcinarte al rayo del sol.

Un sueño! Inmenso e imposible de conocer ni cerca de su totalidad. Recomiendo la compra del parís museum pass que incluye la entrada a este museo (de igual forma debes reservar tu ticket por la página web del Louvre para asegurar tu entrada)
Um museu muito rica em conhecimento, são mais de 30k obras, um dia é pouco para conhecer, tem escuta guiada aconselho ir na parte da manhã.

El Louvre es espectacular. Imposible abarcarlo en una sola jornada, o incluso en varias. Puede ser abrumador si no se tiene esto en cuenta. Las multitudes de turistas contribuyen a esta sensación, y dificultan la apreciación de las obras expuestas.

An Kultur kaum zu überbieten. Was für ein beeindruckendes Museum.
Man sollte allerdings nicht den Fehler begehen und Montags dort hin gehen. An Montagen sind andere Museen in Paris geschlossen, daher ist der ohnehin schon überfüllte Louvre noch voller.
Alles intensiv zu betrachten ist an einem Tag unmöglich.
So viel Geschichte, einfach toll.
Bei einem Besuch in Paris sollte man dieses Stück Kultur mitnehmen.

Un lieu incontournable à découvrir au moins une fois dans sa vie. Riche en histoire et en œuvres emblématiques, il offre une immersion unique dans l’art et la culture à travers les siècles. La diversité des collections est impressionnante, allant de l’Antiquité aux œuvres plus modernes, avec des pièces mondialement connues. Même si la visite peut être fatigante en raison de la taille du musée et de l’affluence, l’expérience reste exceptionnelle et mémorable.

Un lugar radiante y magnífico. Cada pasillo tiene una historia única, donde al cerrar los ojos puedes imaginar lo vivido en cada época. El museo es solo un espacio para explorar lo grandioso de todas las culturas de la humanidad💜

Consiglio di acquistare i biglietti in anticipo e inclusi di audioguida. È molto facile seguire le indicazioni e la mappa. Meglio farsi un itinerario in anticipo per evitare di uscire più di 3 volte. Per accedere da una parte all’altra bisogna ripresentare i biglietti e puó essere fatto massino tre volte. Conviene fare prima i vari piani di uno stesso lato e poi cambiare. Alcune aree sono chiuse, meglio controllare prima.

The Louvre Museum was an absolutely amazing experience, and if you have the chance, you should definitely visit!

However, there’s one thing you should absolutely avoid: riding the so-called “tuk-tuks.”

At first, they offer what seems like a cheap price, but once you arrive at your destination, they suddenly show you a ridiculous price chart and claim that the fare is actually for that destination and charged per person. In the end, they aggressively demand about four times more than the original price they mentioned.

If you want to ruin your wonderful trip to Paris in a single moment, then go ahead and try the “tuk-tuks.”

루브르 박물 관은 너무 좋은 경험입니다. 가능하면 꼭 방문하세요!
하지만 절대로 하지 말아야 할게 있습니다.
바로 “툭툭” 이라고 부르는 이동 수단을 탑승하는겁니다. 처음에 싼 가격으로 가격을 말해주고 도착지에가면 말도안 되는 가격 표를 보여주면서 이 도착지는 이가격이다 그리고 인당 가격이다 라면서 처음이야기
했던 가격에 4배 정도 되는 가격을 막무가네로 달라고 합니다.
기분 좋은 파리 여행을 한순간에 완전 망치고 싶다면 “툭툭”을 타보시면 그렇게 될겁니다..😫

Der Louvre in Paris ist riesig. Wenn man sich wirklich mit den Ausstellungsstücken befassen will, schafft man ihn nicht an einem Tag. Als Tourist hilft da eigentlich nur, sich auf bestimmte Bereiche zu konzentrieren. Diese Idee haben allerdings viele, dementsprechend ist es dort dann auch recht voll, aber an der Mona Lisa oder der Venus von Milo führt halt kein Weg vorbei. Wir hatten ein wenig Glück, deshalb konnten wir unser Programm durchziehen. Die vier Stunden vergingen sehr schnell und ich bin von dem Museum richtig beeindruckt. Es ist ein absolutes Muss bei einem Besuch in Paris. Unbedingt vorbuchen, die Hoffnung auf Tageskarten ist nicht sehr hoch.
En tant que parisienne on en peut plus de ne meme plus pouvoir accéder aux musées de notre ville. Y a trop de monde et jamais de billets dispo. Trop de touristes c est l usine.

人潮很多，但是井然有序，只要有預訂，提早半小時過來金字塔入口旁邊找到對應的排隊位置，不需要擔心

看到蒙娜麗莎的人潮真的會嚇到，但是慢慢地前面的人從左右出去之後，後面的人就可以往前推進，還是可以靠近她的

鎮館三寶之外，推薦雕塑區，Denon館區值得花時間細細品味

館內有餐廳，小吃吧，很大的禮品區，到出口之前還有不少品牌的店，當年想離開來到倒三角區到真正站在外面馬路，還要半小時；建議至少規劃4小時的時間，才不會太趕

L'expérience est très sympa un peu long et plutôt labyrinthe malgré le plan je penses qu'il y'a des parties que nous n'avons pas réussi à trouver. Tour assez conséquent et long mais les œuvres d'art en valent le coup. Les œuvres les plus connues et surtout la Joconde sont difficiles d'accès.

O Museu do Louvre é fantástico, a visita é imperdível. Compramos os ingressos online, não conseguimos comprar os horários mais cedo, fomos no horário das 10h30. Entramos pela entrada que se conecta com o metrô, eles formam a fila conforme horário de ingresso. O museu é muito grande, tem uma boa estrutura, mas não tem como visitar com calma em um dia. Vimos os principais quadros e queremos voltar.

Muy grande, mejor organizado que la Torre Eiffel. Tienes consignas para dejar las maletas de forma gratuita. Recomiendo ir pronto, a primera hora, ya que aunque van rápido las filas que están programadas por hora, para ver luego el museo es mejor hacerlo sin tanta gente.

Wat een prachtig museum. Het is enorm groot en daardoor ook wel overweldigend. Je kunt nooit alles in 1x zien. Dus even goed de plattegrond erbij en kiezen wat je wilt zien. De vertrekken van Napoleon zijn een aanrader om te bekijken.
Tickets moet je vooraf kopen via de site van het Louvre. Voor jongeren tot 18 en studenten tot 26 is het gratis.
Prijs viel ons erg mee.

Museu incrível, não tem o que falar, mas a quantidade de pessoas atrapalha um pouco a experiência. É realmente muito difícil ter uma imersão profunda nas obras, pois são muitas, muitas mesmo! O ideal é passar nas principais, tirar fotos, se aprofundar somente nas obras mais notáveis e mesmo assim vão 4 horas com facilidade (e mesmo assim você não vai ver tudo)!

J’ai adoré mon experience. L’entrée sans billets est plutôt rapide ( environ 15mn d’attente ) et l’intérieur magnifique, personne ne se marche dessus, on peut apprécier la grandeur historique du lieu pendant autant de temps que l’on souhaite. J’ai adoré l’expérience

Compramos ingressos com antecedência e chegamos meia hora antes da entrada. A fila é grande, mas muito bem organizada. O museu é muito grande, com muitas atrações e obras magníficas. Sugiro ir com tempo, pois vc fica muitas horas dentro do museu. Não deixem de ver as relíquias do castelo medieval ( onde o Louvre foi erguido e a obra Victoire de Samothrace ( a 2° obra mais visitada no Louvre, após Monalisa). Além disso, após a visitação, há um shopping que dá pra vc fazer refeição com uma praça de alimentação com muitas opções.

Jedno z największych muzeów. Zbiory sztuki malarstwa i rzeźby niesamowite!
Trzeba mieć na prawdę dużo czasu żeby zobaczyć wszystko! Żeby zobaczyć choćby tylko najważniejsze potrzeba minimum 3h czasu! Bardzo dużo chodzenia, w tym dużo chodzenia po schodach (windy też są dostępne)

Ha....le Louvre ! The musée mondial ! Réservation obligatoire ( 9h30 est le bon horaire ) prévoir de l'attente et sans bagages pour plus de fluidité ! Attention toilettes dames avec beaucoup de monde ! Sinon que du bonheur , un peu le bazard avec le " selfie " devant la joconde ! Mais en jouant des " coudes " ça passe crème !!

最高でした。展示品は非常に多く、1日で全部見るのは不可能に思えるので、見たい展示物を予め絞って行くのが良いと思いました。
朝はまだ比較的混んでいないので、朝一のチケットを取って、1番混むモナリザを最初に観に行くのが良いと思います。

Esta increíble por fuera y por dentro. Puedes pasar horas y horas recorriendo todo su interior. Te recomiendo comprar tu ticket desde antes y reservar, te vas a evitar mucha fila. Procura ir descansada porque si es muy abrumante.

Лувр произвел огромное впечатление — масштаб музея просто невероятный. Даже за один день невозможно увидеть всё, но главные шедевры, такие как Мона Лиза или Венера Милосская, однозначно стоит увидеть вживую.

В выходные было много людей, поэтому лучше приходить как можно раньше. Несмотря на толпы, атмосфера там особенная, а само место наполнено историей и искусством.

Определенно рекомендую — это обязательное место для посещения в Париже, даже если у вас есть всего один день.
Вы просто обязаны посетить этот музей хоть раз в жизни 10/10. Но лучше это делать с раннего утра и не туристический сезон, тогда вы сможете насладиться им.

Superbe musée, à faire absolument. Pour une visite complète, prévoir plusieurs jours.
L'Égypte, bien sûr, la Joconde, Eugène Delacroix avec la Liberté guidant le peuple, etc., et beaucoup plus à vous de découvrir.
Continuer ensuite par la remontée vers l'Arc de Triomphe, en passant par le jardin des Tuileries, puis les Champs-Élysées.

와 다보는데 3-4시간 걸렸네요
너무너무 지쳤어요
그치만 볼 가치가 충분히 있는 박물관!
개인적으로 쉴리관은 별로 취향인 작품이 없었고
드농관은 유명한 작품이 많아서 첫번째로 보면 좋을 것 같고
리슐리외관은 건물 자체가 아름답고 조각상이나 함무라비법전 등 볼거리가 많아 마지막에 둘러보기 좋았습니다

Dünyaca ünlü bir müze. Bileti önceden de alsanız bile yine sıra bekliyorsunuz. Biletsiz sıraya girdik. Yarım saat sürdü beklememiz gerekti sıra hemen geldi. Alt katta bilet otomatları var. Türkçe hiç bir broşür harita maalesef yok. Çok büyük bir müze. Gitmeden önce internette ki müze haritasına bakın ve eserler hakkında bilgi edinin. İslam eserleri kısmında Türkiye’den Osmanlı dönemine ait eserler de var.

İnanılmaz güzellikte ve büyüklükte bir müze..Müze çok büyük ve her şeyi görmek, daha doğrusu olabildiğince çok şeyi görmek çok zaman alıyor ve doğrusunu söylemek gerekirse bazen de mümkün olmuyor..Yönlendirme tabelaları yetersiz..Louvre’u ziyaret etmek muhteşem bir Paris deneyimi.. Mona Lisa’dan Mısır hazinelerine kadar çok sayıda sanat koleksiyonu var. Çok fazla yürüyüş olduğu için rahat ayakkabılar giymeyi unutmayın ve oraya erken gitmek kalabalıklardan kaçınmanıza yardımcı
Her zaman önceden bilet alarak gitmek akılcı bir tercih..Paris ziyaretine mutlaka görmek gerekiyor...

Muzej je ogroman,predivno uredjen. Tavanice oslikane i sa pozlacenim figurama. Eksponati su odlicno prezentovani i pilutokaz do eksponata je odlican. Najposeceniji su Mona Liza ,Miloska Venera i Luka iz Samotrake.Slika i skulpture koje sam pomenula su od ulaza desno,u krilu Denon. Karte je najbolje kupiti on line,bar nedelju dana ranije. Na dan posete dodjite 1 sat ranije,stanite u red i bicete u tacno vreme sa karte ,u muzeju. Neverovatno iskusvo obilaska  ovog predivnog muzeja. Obavezno ga treba posetiti. Blizu muzeja je trzni centar Carusel,sa velikim brojem prodavnica poznatih brendova. Tu se nalazi i prodavnica muzeja Luvr

Magnifique musée, un véritable labyrinthe d'oeuvre d'art pour tous les styles avec de grands incontournable comme la Joconde, la Vénus de Milo, les salles mésopotamiennes et égyptiennes... Un régal pour les yeux. Prévoir sa journée et de bonne chaussures on ne voit pas le temps passer...

Лувр - впечатляющий, чарующий и при этом такой, каким я себе всегда его представляла.
Тысячи экспонатов - от древнеегипетских саркофагов до апартаментов Наполеона III - представлены на огромной территории, разделенной на 5 уровней.
У мены были большие опасения по поводу чрезмерной загруженности музея: каждый слышал о километровых очередях на вход и толпах народа около Джоконды. Но, соблюдая всего несколько правил, вы можете попасть в почти пустой музей:

1. Билеты нужно брать заранее на любой день со среды по пятницу: во вторник музей закрыт, в понедельник закрыт Версаль, из-за чего туристы часто выбирают посещение Лувра в этот день, с субботой и воскресеньем, думаю, все понятно. Стоимость билетов на одного взрослого для граждан стран, не-ЕС) - 32€.

2. Временной слот выбирам самый ранний - у меня был билет на 9.00. Часто слышала рекомендации, что в среду и четверг также можно выбирать поздние слоты (после 18.00) и тоже попасть в полупустой музе..

3. Приходим минимум за 30 минут и становимся в очередь на вход у Пирамиды - таким образом, за 10-15 минут до открытия музея за вами уже будет огромная очередь.

4. Если все-таки опоздали,то заходим не с главного входа, а с торца здания, там есть отдельный вход для опоздавших.

5. Обязательно берем карту, с ней проще ориентироваться.

6. В первую очередь идем в зал №711 - чтобы посмотреть шедевр Леонардо да Винчи с первого ряда, не пробираясь через толпу, уже через 45 минут после открытия музея такой возможности у вас не будет.

7. После этого осматриваем крыло Денон на первом ярусе - там в основном представлена живопись французских, итальянских и испанских художников, в том числе Эжена Делакруа, Караваджо, Франсиско де Гойя.

8. После этого осматриваем второй уровень - там представлена преимущественно французская скульптура.

9. Затем можем спокойно осматривать нулевой и подвальном уровни. Нулевой уровень посвящен древним цивилизациям - греческой, египетской, ближневосточной. На подвальном уровне царит прохлада и представлены жемчужины Средневековья. На этих уровнях загруженность меньше.

10. Отводим 2-3 часа времени для спокойного осмотра экспозиции.

11. Ищем выход (это тоже оказалось не совсем просто, указатели очень странные).

Таким образом, я получила огромное удовольствие от посещения одного из самых известных музеев мира, без суеты и очередей.

Le plus grand musée du monde. Incontestablement un lieu a visiter. Il ne faut pas se décourager par la file d'attente interminable à l'entrée, car sur place, le musée est tellement gigantesque que l'on peut profiter de chaque section si l'on choisit bien ses périodes de fréquentation (éviter la Joconde en heure de pointe pour aller sur les sites moins visités)

平日の10時に予約して行きました。
入り口が2箇所ありますが両方確認してどっちも並んでるなーと言った印象です。
少し早くきたので、観察していると意外と当日券の人が早く入れてる印象でした。
10時予約で、10時５分くらいには入れたと思います。
4時間くらい色々見て回りましたが、職員の方が寝ている人もいれば、スマホに集中している人もいて文化の違いを感じで見ていて面白かったです。
作品は見たいものは全て見れたので大満足です。

L’exposition consacrée à Auguste Rodin et Michel-Ange au Musée du Louvre est absolument remarquable.

Le dialogue entre les œuvres des deux artistes est fascinant et permet de mieux comprendre l’influence immense de Michel-Ange sur Rodin. On découvre toute la puissance des corps, l’émotion dans les expressions et le travail exceptionnel du mouvement et de la matière.

La scénographie est élégante et met parfaitement en valeur les sculptures et dessins exposés. Chaque salle donne envie de prendre le temps d’observer les détails et de ressentir la force artistique de ces deux génies.

Une exposition passionnante, enrichissante et très inspirante, aussi bien pour les amateurs d’art que pour les visiteurs curieux. Une très belle expérience au Louvre.

Birçok ülkede çok önemli müzeler gezdim ama Louvre Müzesi kadar etkileyici bir müze görmedim. Kesinlikle 1 tam gün ayrılması gerekiyor. Ya çok uzun zaman öncesinden müzesin sitesinden bilet almanız gerekiyor ya da get your guide gibi sitelerden öncelikli geçiş olan fiyatı biraz daha yüksek biletlerden temin etmeniz lazım… Giriş için minimum 30-40 dk beklemeniz gerekiyor.

Müzede binlerce yıla yayılan birçok farklı medeniyete ait çok önemli eserler bulunuyor. Tabii ki o kadar çok esere rağmen en çok ilgi toplayan eser Mona Lisa tablosu…

Müze çok güzel planlanmış. Dolayısıyla çok rahat geziliyor. Gezerken eşyalarınızı bırakmanız için ücretsiz dolaplar da bulunuyor. İçeride yiyecek ve içecek seçenekleri de var. Gezerken mola verebilirsiniz.
루브르는 루브르였다. 진짜 넓고 볼 게 많다. 바로 스킵하고 1층만 돌았는데, 1층만 다 보는데 몇시간씩 걸렸다. 정확하게는 보다가 다리 아파서 앉아서 쉬고, 보다가 앉아서 쉬고 그랬다. 그림도 많지만 엄청 화려한 궁전도 많아서 볼 게 진짜 많았다. 힘들어서 다른 층은 생략했다.

Bello lugar, gigante y lleno de historia
Hay que ir con zapatos cómodos ya que se camina bastante. Es como un laberinto las primeras horas.
Hay folletos con el.detalle de cada piso y aun así uno se pierde jajjajjajaja
Muchas escaleras asi que hay que ir con tiempo y ganas de caminar muchisimo

Il Louvre è un enorme tempio dell’arte. Io sono entrato dalla Porta dei Leoni, con il mio biglietto comprato online, e devo dire che è stata una mossa vincente: ho aspettato 10 minuti in coda, presentandomi una decina di minuti prima dell’apertura. Da lì è molto facile arrivare alla Gioconda e agli altri capolavori del museo, basta prendere l’ascensore, andare tutto dritto e poi a sinistra. Diciamo che, se si vuole fare una visita generale a tutte le sale, e se ci si organizza bene, circa 3 ore/3 ore e mezza bastano, molte delle opere  sono enormi e occupano molto spazio. Ci sono moltissimi “scorci” per fare tante belle foto alle opere. Unici punti forse un po negativi: dovrebbero esserci più bagni, magari più luoghi di ristoro, anche solo per dell’acqua o macchinette automatiche, il mercoledì la parte del codice di hammurabi è chiusa, quindi comprare un biglietto per poi non vedere nemmeno tutto forse non è una cosa molto giusta. Inoltre, pensavo forse di trovare più opere celebri di quelle che già c’erano. Comunque, un’emozione poter osservare certe opere che compongono i manuali di storia dell’arte dal vivo. Sotto la piramide del Louvre, si trovano anche diversi bei negozi con alcuni souvenir molto interessanti.

Juste magnifique !! Autant les expositions que l architecture, mais WAOUHH.
Ne vous y méprenez pas, ce musée est gigantesque 🫪. Cependant,avec leurs expositions classées, cela rend la visite plus sélective suivant ce que l on veut voir.
Aussi étonner également de connaître l histoire du Louvre lui-même.
Réservation en amont, avec des créneaux horaires.
Des casiers sont sur places.
Petit avertissement : attention sur quelle plateforme vous acheter vos billets
Bonne visite à vous

Der Louvre ist eines der beeindruckendsten Museen der Welt und ein absolutes Highlight in Paris. Neben der berühmten Glaspyramide erwartet einen im Inneren eine riesige Sammlung mit Meisterwerken wie der Mona Lisa, der Venus von Milo oder der Nike von Samothrake.

Das Museum ist allerdings sehr groß und oft stark besucht. Mein Tipp: Tickets unbedingt vorher online buchen, sonst muss man lange warten oder bekommt kein passendes Zeitfenster mehr.

Trotz der vielen Besucher ein unvergessliches Erlebnis und definitiv einen Besuch wert!

Piękne muzeum z ogromem dzieł sztuki, które trzeba koniecznie zobaczyć. Polecam spędzić tam dzień, aby być usatysfakcjonowany. Jeżeli macie tylko 3 godziny ( bo mniej czasu nie polecam) to możecie tylko z mapą obejrzeć najważniejsze dzieła. Praktycznie przebiec przez Luwr. Co do godzin odwiwdzin to nie ma złotego środka. Byliśmy o godzinie 14:00 i weszliśmy bez kolejki.

Mutlaka görülmeli, harika bir müze ve şahane eserler.Biletlerinizi erkenden almanızı tavsiye ederim. Ayrıca giriş saatinizden 15-20 dk önce orada olmanız da iyi olur. Giriş saatinize göre sıraya girip içeri alınıyorsunuz.

🎨 🏛️ Museo del Louvre
Hay lugares que impresionan… y después está el Louvre.

Su imponencia y majestuosidad se sienten incluso antes de entrar. La pirámide de vidrio reflejando el cielo parisino ya anticipa que lo que está por venir es extraordinario.

Caminar por sus salas es recorrer siglos de historia, cultura y humanidad. Cada obra tiene una energía única. Desde la enigmática sonrisa de la Mona Lisa hasta esculturas que parecen cobrar vida, todo es arte en su máxima expresión.
Es maravilloso. Es indescriptible.
Y lo más increíble es que ninguna foto logra capturar lo que realmente se siente ahí dentro. No se puede explicar en imágenes… hay que vivirlo, hay que estar ahí, hay que contemplarlo en persona.

El Louvre no es solo un museo. Es una experiencia que te transforma, que te hace sentir pequeño ante tanta grandeza y, al mismo tiempo, profundamente conectado con la historia del mundo.

Hay cosas que no se muestran… se experimentan. ✨🇫🇷

#Louvre #Arte #París #Historia #Cultura #ExperienciaÚnica

Le musée du Louvre est un lieu exceptionnel à visiter à Paris. Les collections sont impressionnantes et permettent de découvrir des œuvres célèbres du monde entier. Le musée est immense et chaque salle réserve de belles découvertes. Malgré l’affluence, l’expérience reste unique. C’est un endroit incontournable pour les amateurs d’art comme pour les curieux.

Ein sehr beeindruckendes Museum mit vielen Ausstellungsstücken. Allerdings sind leider viel zu viele Menschen da, sodass es teils sehr laut und stickig ist. Soweit ich das bemerkt habe gibt's leider auch keine Klimaanlage, sodass es bei warmen Wetter auch im Museum warm wird.

It's always a pleasure to view the priceless works of art at the Louvre. Although I've visited before, it's wonderful to return and admire these incredible masterpieces again. I must say it has become much busier than it was 10 years ago, but the collection remains as impressive as ever. If you're visiting on a hot day, I'd recommend bringing a small fan, as it can get quite warm inside despite being indoors.

Väldigt fint museum men tyvärr väldigt dåligt organiserat på många sätt för att vara ett så populärt museum. Långa köer fast man har förbokat, mycket folk och dålig ventilation, finns inga platser att sitta om man behöver ta en paus och besöka caféet. Väldigt dåligt organiserat särskilt kring Mona Lisa som alla vill se. Extrem trängsel och min dotter blev nästan klämd och bortputtad av vuxna människor.

Avevamo i biglietti fatti online con ingresso alle 11.30. siamo arrivati alle 10.45. File lunghissime e dopo un po' gli addetti ci hanno suggerito di lasciar perdere perché ancora dovevano entrare quelli con prenotazione per le 9.00. Ci hanno consigliato di riprovare nel pomeriggio. Secondo me fanno cassa facile vendendo più biglietti di quante persone possano entrare e poi ti dicono di andare via. Serietà zero!

Came on a sunday and this museum is really really big. It was pretty packed that day and a bit warm in the museum, so I suggest wearing comfortable shoes and clothing if deciding to explore around. There's a café within the museum for a quick coffee and a quick bite to eat. Its best if you get tickets in advance to avoid the long lines and wait times. A few buses do run in the area as well so getting there by public transportation wouldn't be too hard. Overall its a great place to go if you're into art and other historical events.

4/5

The most visited museum in the world, with encyclopedic collections spanning from Ancient Egypt to European painting. Seeing the Mona Lisa is iconic, though many, specificially me, every time, are surprised by its small size; and in my view some lesser-known works are even more impactful and finely executed. Overall, only the Hermitage in St. Petersburg, with its vast masterpieces, specifically painting, and imperial setting, surpasses it.

The Louvre is a place everyone wants to visit at least once, but it can be an exhausting and overwhelming experience. It is massive, and the sheer volume of people makes it difficult to navigate. If you are in Paris for a short time, I wouldn't recommend it unless you have a full day to dedicate to it.

As an art lover, I found the experience with the Mona Lisa a bit disappointing; you have to wait in a massive line just to see it from a distance for a few seconds. In my opinion, there are countless other masterpieces in the museum that deserve much more attention and can be appreciated with more calm. It’s a world class museum, but be prepared for a workout and a lot of crowds. Planning is essential!

A well laid out museum, though honestly not my favourite. Several rooms were closed for renovation, which was not great and the galleries of art were not always to my taste. The mesopotamian collection was fantastic however.  All personal choice 😀

The Louvre is one of those places that truly lives up to its reputation. It’s not just a museum — it feels like an entire world of art, history, and culture. The building itself is impressive.
Inside, the collection is massive: paintings, sculptures, ancient artifacts, royal rooms, and iconic works you’ve known your whole life. You genuinely need a full day to explore it properly, and even then you’ll only scratch the surface. Every gallery leads to another surprise.
The only real downside is the waiting. Even with a ticket booked for a specific time slot, the line took almost an hour to get through, which can be frustrating, especially on busy days.

Do remember to reserve beforehand. Their educator ticket only applies to art, but does not restrict to Europe (US may also enter for free with identities).
It was just too big and crowded inside, especially when it came to Mona Lisa… there was no order. People in front row could stay as much time as they want and the whole line would not move. They should better organize the flow/crowd.

You got to see NIKE as well, besides Mona Lisa. It was also very stunning. Also the napoleon crowns and diamonds.

The inner decorations were amazing

Other than that and a few paintings, there was nothing so special. Probably I was not very good at appreciating the arts or neither do I have good knowledge.

There was special night admission on Wednesday and Friday and they are open until 9:45.

You can wait for sunset and get really good photo with dark blue theme and lightning pyramids.

It’s just too large and spread out (things mentioned above are concentrated though). Be prepared to walk a lot. Also, don’t get too ambitious in seeing everything. Do some work and plans ahead.

Even if you don't like museums, you must visit this one! Don't just look at the exhibits, look up at the incredible decor of this incredible place! The details in the ceilings and walls are alone historic and artistic treasures. Food is not so easy to get, so remember to eat first! Mona Lisa is there, but everyone is underwhelmed by its small size!
The museum is fantastic, but staff can be rude. Make sure to arrive early due to people traffic. Definitely recommend more crowd control and queue in some areas like the Mona Lisa exposition.
It was pretty good but I was only there for two hours. Would recommend at least three hours to enjoy everything. mona Lisa room got very busy very quickly

The Louvre is huge! I wish it had ac but understand can't happen. Lol make reservations ahead of time. Wear comfortable shoes. I'd get a guide if you want to know more history on the art. Mona Lisa is so packed so be careful as people push.

Amazing collection of art, but entirely too crowded.  They don’t have a clue about crowd control or managing orderly access to the exhibits.  We pre-purchased tickets for a 10am small group tour and the guide was great.  They definitely need to improve the viewing of the Mona Lisa and it would be so easy.

There was a lot of beautiful art but it's incredibly crowded and loud, almost to the point it makes the experience unenjoyable. I loved sitting in the sculpture garden, that was the most peaceful part.
You really need a lot of patience to stand in line and get inside, but it’s worth it at least once in a lifetime. The Louvre is unique and full of masterpieces — definitely an experience to remember.

Amazing collection and spaces. However, you should plan well in advance what and where to see. The galleries are vast, crowded and can be confusing to orient yourself. If you missed something it can take quite some time to go back.
We rented audio guide, but it brought little additional value beyond what was already written in descriptions.
Double check if the area you care to visit is not under renovation, that is highlighted in a map.

This is a very touristy site ... even with tickets took 45 mins to get in. It is very nice inside with a large volume of items to view and enjoy however the sheer amount of people really takes away from enjoying the artwork.
Seeing the Mona Lisa was like a zoo, lots of shuffling to get a position, not a particularly fun experience especially if you're with a group of people. It felt a little like Disneyland.
If I visit Paris again, which is very likely, I'd probably not visit the Louvre again. I suppose I am glad I went one time though.
Book tickets ahead.
If you are lucky the queue for spot ticket will be short and might get early entry then the ones who booked online

Went for birthday (mid july) very busy, however, great art, and disabled badge free entry, as well as skip mona lisa queue (better photo opportunities than queue). Gift shop was good, macaroons from cafe are amazing. Long walks and lots of stairs, lifts were available but 1 or 2 not working for some areas.

A very big museum. You won’t be able to cover it in one day, so choose your favorite topic and go straight there. If you start at 9:00, you’ll have the chance to avoid the crowds.

You can get a map at the entrance, which is a good idea unless you enjoy getting lost. There are also restaurants and shops inside the building, but they are usually a bit overpriced.
Unfortunately the Louvre is closed today on Tuesday. And tomorrow we are leaving Paris. But it’s still a great photo spot — Instagrammable.

The Louvre is truly amazing, and as an artist I deeply appreciate the masterpieces and the history it holds. It’s an unforgettable experience. However, when it’s overcrowded, the long lines and packed halls really take away from the magic—at that point, it doesn’t feel worth the wait. Best to plan your visit early or during quieter hours.

Es una experiencia increíble, todo aquello que alguna vez viste en libros o en fotografías, está frente tus ojos. El museo no es solo la mona lisa, hay muchas exposiciones de todas las épocas. Impresiona incluso si no te gusta la historia.
Las 4 estrellas es por la gestión tanto fuera como dentro del museo, muchas veces tardan en abrir el museo o no respetan las filas.
Todo lo interior vale mucho la pena.

Dass ist ein muss. Leider schaffen wir's erst beim zweiten Paris besuchen rein. Es ist sehr viel los und bei uns waren auch sehr viele Räume gesperrt darum die 4 Sterne. Man sollte auf jeden Fall gleich in der früh hin dann wird man nicht so geschoben.

Wunderbares Erlebnis, die Kunstsammlung ist sehr interessant und vielfältig. Wenn man sich nicht die meiste zeit anstellen möchte. Sollte man eine geführte Tour mit schnellem Einlass buchen. Danach kann man das Musseum noch alleine erkunden.
Maravilloso. Es increíble la experiencia. Hay que ir con tiempo porque está súper concurrido. Adentro hay locales para comer y tomar. Hay locales para comprar recuerdos.

Le Louvre reste un musée incontournable à visiter.
Le système de réservation est pratique pour avoir un créneau d'entrée, encore faut-il respecter tout le protocole de sécurité, ce qui rallonge l'attente. Pour un créneau à 9h30, nous avions dû patienter 30 minutes pour descendre sous la pyramide et attendre encore pour entrer dans une des ailes.
Quant aux œuvres, je conseille d'aller admirer La Joconde le plus tôt possible. C'est dommage que l'espace consacré ne soit pas plus grand et cela ne permet pas non plus aux autres œuvres d'être étudiées correctement (trop de monde pour rester)
J'avais oublié que le palais du Louvre était aussi grand et avec tant d'escaliers !
Ayant visité ce musée à plusieurs reprises, cela reste une belle visite mais je trouve qu'il manque une petite touche de modernité dans la présentation des œuvres.

Από τα πιο εμβληματικά μουσεία του κόσμου. Το επισκεφτήκαμε το πρωί και ενώ είχαμε κλείσει ηλεκτρονικά εισιτήρια περιμέναμε 50 λεπτά να μπούμε από την είσοδο carrousel στο μετρό. Προτιμήστε να το επισκεφτείτε τις απογευματινές ώρες τις ημέρες που λειτουργεί μέχρι αργά το βράδυ. Εάν μπορείτε, αποφύγετε την Τετάρτη που γίνεται χαμός, γιατί κάθε Τρίτη το μουσείο είναι κλειστό. Γενικά, χάνεσαι πολύ εύκολα. Ο χάρτης δεν βοηθάει και πολύ, καθώς πολλές αίθουσες είναι κλειστές και μπερδεύεσαι. Οι διάδρομοι είναι ασφυκτικά γεμάτοι από κόσμο, ειδικά μπροστά σε γνωστά εκθέματα, χάνοντας την εμπειρία θέασης των έργων τέχνης. Χρειάζεσαι τουλάχιστον 4 ώρες για να δεις τα βασικα.

Müzenin Avrupa Birliği vatandaşları ile diğer vatandaşların arasında fiyat farklı uygulamasından dolayı yıldızını kırıyorum. Müzenin kalitesi, görevlilerin yardımları ise çok iyiydi. Bir günü sabahtan akşama kadar ayırmanız güzel bir gezi için gerekli.

Tres beau (et immense musee) une mention particuliere pour l'aile des 5 continents et l'art de l'islam, magnifique! Parcontre a l'entrée l'accueil, par le personnel du musée,n'est pas un modele de bienveillance (doux euphemisme). Il est bien stipulé sur les pancartes qu'il faut etre respectueux avec l'ensemble du personnel du musée (ce qui me semble bien normal) mais l'inverse est aussi vrai!

Niesamowite muzeum. Sam budynek robi ogromne wrażenie, nie wspominając o zgromadzonych tam dziełach sztuki. Jeden cały dzień to za mało, żeby zobaczyć wszystko. Do Mona Lisy oczywiście kolejka i tłok, ale w innych częściach już luzniej. Dziedziniec z rzeźbami w skrzydle Richelieu przepiękny.
Na minus: ogromny tłok, a przez to bardzo duże kolejki do toalety oraz brak miejsca w głównym holu, żeby spokojnie wypić kawę i odpocząć między kolejnymi skrzydłami.

Bonne expérience au musée du Louvres, conseillé de le faire tout au long de la journée ou plusieurs fois étant très grand. Un peu de modernité avec des bornes interactives serait je pense intéressant à travailler pour un public plus jeune.

Attente a l'entrée malgré des billets pris a l'avance.Tres beau et immense musée.Tellement de belles choses à voir,prévoir plusieurs heures de visite.Point très négatif pour voir la joconde,les gens se poussent tous,collés serrés..Organiser une file d'attente afin de passer chacun son tour serait vraiment plus qu'appreciable et permettrait aussi d'apprécier l'œuvre dans de bonnes conditions.

J’ai beaucoup aimé le Louvre, c’est incroyable. Cela peut être épuisant de tout voir en une seule visite, donc mon conseil est d’y aller plusieurs fois et de prendre son temps si vous aimez vraiment l’art.

I really enjoyed the Louvre; it’s amazing. It can be exhausting to see everything in one visit, so my advice is to go multiple times and take your time if you love art.

Leider viel zu viele Besucher, schade gibt es keine Einlassbeschränkung....
Kunstwerke kommen dadurch gar nicht Recht zur Geltung.

Haben 2 Stunden Führung gemacht, dadurch rascherer Eintritt.

Bello ma troppo dispersivo.
Ho fatto lo stesso corridoio 2 volte perché non capivo da che lato uscire.
Consiglierei di tracciare un percorso da seguire

Por algo es un icono de París y probablemente el museo más conocido e importante del mundo.

Las obras de arte que tiene, obviamente entre ellas la Mona Lisa y la virgen de las rocas de Da Vinci,, la Venus de Milo, la victoria alada de Samotracia, el código de Hammurabi, los aposentos de Napoleón III, las esculturas de Canova y Miguel Ángel, entre muchas, muchas otras, no sólo son impresionantes sino un referente de la cultura del mundo.
El edificio además, tanto por dentro como por fuera es un placer de observar y caminar.
Es sin exagerar un imperdible no sólo de París, sino del mundo.

No todo es positivo, sin embargo.
- La muy lógica impaciencia del personal. El museo cierra la mayoría de los días a las 6 pm, y desde las 5:30 ya nos estaban corriendo. Entiendo que te quieren fuera a las 6, pero muchas veces es la única ocasión en tu vida que estarás ahí.
- El exceso de visitantes que somos, hace que en muchos momentos sea difícil caminar y ver las obras. Entre semana y en la parte inicial o final del día es la mejor alternativa.
- El costo. En 2026 cuesta $32 EUR por adulto.

Tips relevantes.
- Hay días de la semana que cierra a las 9 PM, vale la pena investigar para aprovecharlo mucho mejor
- Los niños de 11 años y menos no pagan, y los menores de 18 pagan una tarifa menor.

Хороший опыт посещение. Рекомендую приходит за 1,5-2 часа до открытия, что бы быть одним из первых в очереди на вход. Далее рекомендую приходить на самое открытие, чтобы была возможность с меньшим количеством людей обойти все воплощения искусства. Внутри есть буфет и сувенирные лавки.

Het Louvre is een must see, ga naar de Mona Lisa, draai je om en kijk naar het enorme meesterwerk het huwelijk bij Canaa van Veronese, ga vervolgens weer terug de gang op en bekijk de andere schilderijen van da Vinci zonder drukte. Mijn favoriete gallerij is altijd de toegepaste kunst 500-1800, helaas was ern groot deel afgesloten wegens renovatie.

Un must à voir et revoir et re-revoir… c’est splendide 🤩🤩🤩
Pas étonnant que tous accourent voir les œuvres des maîtres de la peinture, de la sculpture, les splendeurs des appartements de Napoléon, les pierres et le d’objets qui parlent depuis l’Antiquité…
Bémol : un monde de fou, une orientation de visite pas très claire. A prévoir : des suggestions de circuits dans des sens différents, des audio guides avec des suggestions de circuit et une brasserie du côté de la Cour carrée. Elle est magnifique et sert juste de passage pour quitter le musée. Le différend avec la Mairie de Paris doit être réglé au plus vite pour faire circuler les visiteurs plus facilement.
Les audio guidés pourraient aider à faire découvrir d’autres œuvres du musée. Y ajouter le plan de visite et activer la localisation.
Conseil : entrée via Richelieu pour réduire le temps d’attente pour entrer.

הביקורת היא על ביקור יחד עם תינוקת.
הטוב הצוות מאוד שירותי ונעים לעזור. קבלו פנינו בנעימים עם העגלה של נתנו לנו לעבור ללא תור! למרות שטעינו וניסינו לבוא מהכניסה מהמטרו בסוף נכנסנו מהכניסה הראשית. הלכנו יום חמישי בצהרים. היה עמוס והתחיל לרדת בעומס משעה שתיים שלוש. הלא טוב, יש הרבה מדרגות המעליות נסתרות בצורה מיוחדת ללא שילוט מתאים. ולכל הממליצים לשאול צוות לא בכל מקום יש את מי לשאול. על מנת לצאת מהמוזיאון אין יציאה אלא צריך ללכת את כל המסלול. זה מאוד מעייף. אין מקום מספיק לשבת ולאכול היה נחמד עם כל המקום הענקי היה מקום לשבת. במקום לא ממש מתאים לדתיים כי יש הרבה פסלים וחוסר צניעות. דבר נוסף שלא ידענו זה שיש חדרים שפתוחים לפי ימים וזה מצער כי בתקופה שהלכנו כמעט על מה שרציתי היה סגור ולא ידעתי על הפרט הזה. הלכנו למצרים קומה 0 ולמסמפטומיה. אין ספק שזה מרשים. חסרון מהותי זה של מוזיאון כזה גדול הייתי מצפה לכתוביות באנגלית שיסבירו אבל כל הכתוביות היו בצרפתית! לצערי לא לקחתי את האזניות והתחרטתי על כך. בכל מקרה מאוד מענין ההתחלה של מצרים היתה פלוץ מעניינת אבל מהחצי היה מאוד מרשים וחזותי. לא ראינו את החלק השני של מצרים שהיה סגור בקומה 1. סך הכל למצרים לקח כשעתיים ועוד שעה פלוס למנוחה ועוד שעה פלוס למסמפטומיה.

Az épület nagyon impozáns, könnyen meg lehet találni mindent. Van elég büfé, a mosdók aránylag tiszták. Mi tegnap mentünk, 12:00-re volt jegyünk, de így is egy órát sorba kellett állni mire bejutottunk. Rengeteg ember van bent folyamatosan, sajnos ez kicsit elveszi az élményt az igazi múzeumozástól.

Bellissimo museo ma molto molto caotico. Partiamo dal fatto che non basterebbe una settimana per visitarlo tutto, quindi vi consiglio di creare un itinerario di ciò che siete sicuri di voler guardare. Se arrivate poco prima dell'ingresso del vostro biglietto troverete poca fila (soprattutto se entrate dal centro commerciale), arrivati dentro vi consiglio vivamente di scaricare la mappa e di andare a vedere la Gioconda prima che ci sia così tanta fila da non vederla neanche con il binocolo. Alcune sale le abbiamo trovate chiuse senza alcun motivo, le indicazioni non sono molto chiare. Belle le opere, ovviamente, ma è tutto gestito un po' male.
Vi consiglio di tenere da parte almeno 3 ore per la Gioconda, la Venere di Milo, la libertà che guida il popolo, lo scriba seduto, amore e psiche, Nike di Samotracia, l'incoronazione di Napoleone e qualche altra opera più importante.
Se avete meno di 26 anni e siete residenti nello Spazio Economico Europeo il biglietto è gratuito.
Es bonito, preferentemente hay que comprar los boletos en linea para pasar rápido,  es muy concurrido, llevar zapatos cómodos si se quiere recorrer todo el museo

Endlich haben wir es geschafft in den Louvre zu gehen. Sollte man mal gemacht heben und die Sensation Mona Lisa anschauen. Auch wenn ich enttäuscht war wie klein das Gemälde ist, war es dennoch schön anzuschauen.
Drei Flügel komplett einzuschenken war uns am Ende zu viel. Wir haben nur die für uns interessante Kunst geschafft und den Rest dann auf nächstes Mal vertagt.

Trotz online gebuchten Tickets mit Zeitslot leider sehr lange Wartezeit. Kn unserem Fall über 45min. Ausstellung an sich sehr interessant und informativ. Die zurechtfindung mit dem Übersichtsplan ist sehr Gewöhnungsbedürftig und sollte nachgebessert werden!

Beaucoup de salles fermées en même temps. Dommage. Il faut revoir l'accès à la Joconde ..
Sinon musée très varié. Il faudrait clarifier le plan car c'est un peu difficile à suivre.
Aussi, il faudrait offrir de l'eau car beaucoup font des malaises avec la chaleur.
Groot, druk maar ook erg indrukwekkend. De collectie is te groot om gewoon 'op je gemak' te bekijken. Het gebouw op zich is al een kunstwerk. Zeker de moeite waard.

Magnífico, mas perde em organização em diversos quesitos, incluindo a gigantesca fila de entrada das 14h e que terminou por volta das 14:40, além da pessima sinalização
Deixa a desejar em algumas obras com descrição apenas em francês e, em outras, sem descrição visível alguma, o que acho um absurdo
Acho natural ter um certo espaço para visualizar a Monalisa, mas já tem um parapeito e, mais atrás, uma outra fila muito longe que mal permite ver o quadro
좋은경험하고갑니다. 하루로 다 관람하긴 어렵네요. 사람 너무많아서 힘들었어요. 제대로 관람하려면 이틀은 잡아야할듯! 가이드 꼭 동행하세요 대기 없이 바로 입장했고 설명 들으면서 관람하니 재미있어요.

Echt viel zu sehen. Die Mona Lisa ist natürlich stark besucht.  Es gibt ein Geheimtipp um schneller in den Louvre zu kommen: es gibt den Hintereingang der Gleichzeitig der Ausgang ist, dort gibt es keine Wartezeit

Er was blijkbaar personeelstekort waardoor er een paar afdelingen gesloten waren. Terwijl dit niet aangegeven stond op hun site noch op de qr code die je kon scannen. Het is jammer want we betalen de volle prijs en hebben niet alles kunnen bezichten. Op zich een mooi museum en valt er veel te bezichtigen. Op sommige plaatsen is het druk vooral waar de groepen staan met hun gids. En het kan er luidruchtig zijn.

Fuimos un viernes a las 16:30 con entrada ya comprada. El acceso fue muy fácil no había fila. La muestra es impresionante🤯 Hay muchísimas salas y mucho por recorrer. Si sos alguien que le interesa el arte te recomiendo sacar la entrada temprano y quedarte todo el día ahí porque hay muchísimo para ver, y podes entrar con algo para comer pero obviamente no comer en las salas ( y también hay lugares para tomar algo adentro del museo ).
👎Si venís a ver La Mona Lisa te vas a llevar una decepción porque se podía ver pero aproximadamente a una distancia de 10 metros masomenos, lo que es una vergüenza a mi parecer ya que se paga una entrada considerablemente cara en relación a demás atracciones de París. Y que la atracción principal del museo no se pueda apreciar de un poco más cerca es una mala decisión de los que dirigen el museo.
Igual tenes unas obras más de Da Vínci por fuera de ese salón que si se pueden ver y apreciar de más de cerca🙂

Heel mooi museum met prachtige schilderijen. Ik raad zeker aan om vooraf tickets te kopen. En nog een tip: ons werd verteld dat je het beste via de metro ingang kan gaan. Dan ga je niet door de bekende ingang, maar het is daar veel rustiger. De Mona Lisa was ook leuk, met haar mysterieuze glimlach weet ze heel veel bezoekers te trekken. Het was heel druk daar, en ik weet helaas niet of er een tijd is dat het rustig is. Wij gingen in ieder geval tot sluitingstijd, dus denk eind van de middag, en toen was het zo druk als je op de foto ziet. Gelukkig kan je je een beetje naar voren duwen, want de eerste 2 meter stonden de mensen nog niet massaal in elkaar nek te hijgen, maar daarna stond het muurvast😂. Respect voor de mensen die aan het begin van de dag bij opening naar binnen zijn gegaan en toen meteen naar de Mona Lisa zijn gestormd, want ik zou niet weten hoe ik daar weg zou moeten komen. In ieder geval waren de andere schilderijen ook mooi, en dus ook de moeite waard. Echt een museum waar je wat mij betreft eigenlijk wel een keertje moet komen.

Ik hoop dat ik je geholpen heb met deze review😊

Incroyable musée qui nécessite beaucoup de temps pour le visiter (~5 jours), seul bémol, c'est la bagarre pour voir la Joconde en premier rang.

Mis à part ça, on peux y manger et boire donc c'est parfait

Nos ha encantado el museo, está dividido por secciones y tiene una colección enorme de obras de arte, desde esculturas hasta cuadros. Estuvimos unas 4 horas y solo pudimos ver una mitad de todo lo que hay, aunque creemos que estar más tiempo puede resultar pesado. Fuimos un miércoles y había mucha gente, recomiendo coger las entradas anticipadas.

Tickets nur im Internet kaufen (Achtung Fake-Shops, nicht mehr als 25€ zahlen), trotzdem auf Wartezeit einrichten (im Hof) und knallevoll. Die berühmten Bilder sind überlaufen und man kann sie kaum erkennen. Und alles kann man an einem Tag nicht sehen. Trotzdem sehenswert.

Très beau musée, les bâtiments sont magnifiques.
Cependant on a trouvé qu’il était très difficile de s’orienter ce n’est pas super bien indiqué on s’est perdu plusieurs fois.
Les appartements Napoleon pas accessible le jeudi, pourquoi ? Super dommage ça avait l’air vraiment magnifique. Tout comme la Galerie Apollon mais ça je peux comprendre pourquoi elle est fermée.
Et une chose qui n’a rien a voir contre le musée mais les touristes incivilisés c’est insupportable. Tu te fais pousser, passer devant parce qu’ils ne sont pas capables d’attendre comme tout le monde.
