il museo è veramente immenso, io la mia ragazza abbiamo aspettato 15 minuti in fila per entrare, con la prenotazione già effettuata online, e non abbiamo poi avuto problemi a muoverci all'interno. Ovviamente la maggior parte delle persone è lì solo per vedere la Gioconda. Il museo però rappresenta tantissime altre stanze molto vaste e interessanti per gli appassionati. Non è consentito portare cibo e acqua all'interno, io e la mia ragazza ci siamo fermati in uno dei fast-food all'interno. Tutto sommato bella esperienza.

Uno dei più famosi, forse il più famoso di tutti i musei del mondo, a cui però dó umilmente 4 stelle: ma cavoli, togliete le opere di artisti italiani e metà museo puff!, sparisce.. detto questo, il piano Denon con la Gioconda, le nozze di Cana, i dipinti dei francesi ecc. rappresenta appunto di per sé tutto il museo, con ovviamente l'aggiunta di Amore e Psiche fra le sculture. Altri settori, a mio avviso, poco rilevanti. Consiglio di acquistare i biglietti online con fascia d'orario, che è quasi oramai obbligatorio, visto che comunque poi si fa la fila (diverse file per orario, fila per 10.00, fila per 10.30, fila per 11.00, ecc.). Ovviamente StraConsigliato, ma, da italiano, 4 stelle, Tié!

Es un museo impresionante, más grande que el del Vaticano, con muchas muchas cosas interesantes para ver, es decir un imperdible.
La audioguía, es una App es decir un trastorno, la App pesada, en una hora (que había de cola a pesar de haber comprado las entradas con anterioridad), no logré descargar la App y las bases de datos, es decir no pude utilizarla. Gracias a Dios hay señal de celular y pude usar Google Lens para entender algunas obras que me llamaron la atención.
Otro fiasco fué la Gioconda alias la Mona Lisa, un mundo de gente apiñada a varios metros del cuadro, que estaba atrás de un vidrio, es decir no se puede apreciar.
Es tan tan grande que hubo partes del 1er piso que descarté.
Es más difícil salir del museo que entrar, porque hicieron un laberinto de comercios antes de poder ver la Luz del sol, terminamos eligiendo una estación de Subte y salir por el Subte.

Cudowny zbiór dzieł sztuki z całego świata. Jednak najcenniejszym okazem jest Mona Lisa, która nie jest już normalnie dostępna dla szerokiej publiczności. Wszyscy się tłoczą, pilnuje jej ośmioro ochroniarzy, nie ma szans na krótką kontemplację, kilkadziesiąt lat wcześniej obraz wisiał na jednej ze ścian; dziś nawet zdjęcie zrobić trudno. Dobrze przeznaczyć sobie co najmniej kilka godzin, a najlepiej cały dzień. Zwiedzający z biletami ustawiają się do odpowiednich kolejek przed piramidą na kolejne godziny. Dobrze wcześniej zakupić bilety przez internet. Nie kontrolują wody, małą buteleczkę można mieć.

Dla mnie nieporozumienie. Bilety kupione on line na 14:00 to wejście o 14:00. Tak? No nie! Kolejka do wejścia na ponad 1.5 h. W środku tłumy. Zwiedzanie, a raczej przejście nie miało nic wspólnego z przyjemnością. Eksponaty okazałe. Oczywiście. Kawał histori. Jednak po zwiedzaniu Zamku Zwinger w Dreźnie nie robią już wrażenia. Mona Lisa piękna, ale zobaczyć mogą ją najwytrwalsi i najsilniejsi w zapasach. Przepychanki tragedia.
Wniosek jeden. Kultury uczyć powinniśmy się przed obcowaniem z wielką kulturą. W trakcie wychodzi jak wyżej.

El Louvre es espectacular, no solo por las obras, sino por el palacio en sí. Pasear por sus galerías es un viaje en el tiempo. Es un lugar que impone muchísimo en directo. Muy recomendable ir con calzado cómodo y ganas de caminar, porque es gigantesco.
Entrato con guida alle 9 ho visto subito la gioconda ma il museo in sè è dispersivo e difficile da girare ma vedere certe opere compensa

Magnifique,sublime,immense mais qu’un 4 étoiles car le jour où on va au Louvre ils font grève !!! Notre créneau réservé de 9h30 ne sert à rien car tout les créneaux jusqu’à 11h sont passés en même temps, un vrai foutoir….. malgré ça le personnel était sympathique. Et on y retournera car on n’a pas pu tout faire.

事前に作品を確認して何時間で出ようと決めて行きました！
入場列、最初わかりづらかったですが、長蛇の列に負けずに看板を見つけると問題なく並ぶことができました。
長蛇の列でしたが、じわじわと列が動くので許容範囲の待ち時間でした。
夏場だと屋根がないので大変…。
チケットは事前に購入してください！
時間予約もしてください！！

Incredibile.
Molto suggestivo.
Peccato per la complessità nell'orientarsi.
Si potrebbero segnalare meglio le varie stanze.
Mekanın mimarisi ve barındırdığı eserler büyüleyici. Müzenin tek sıkıntısı yönlendirmenin yetersiz olması. İçinde kayboluyorsunuz. Gitmek istediğiniz bölüme ulaşmak oldukça zor.

Lieu remplie d'histoire qui a connue à travers les siècles de nombreuse modifications et nous permet de profiter d'une balade parisienne a contempler ces monuments exceptionnelle.
Je recommande d'être accompagné d'un guide pour cette visite car cela permet de comprendre les chronologies des transformations a travers les règnes différents rois et empereurs.
Je recommande la visite de ce lieu magnifique.

Magnifique musée, incontournable à Paris ! Les collections sont impressionnantes et l’endroit est vraiment grandiose. En revanche, le lieu est tellement vaste qu’il est parfois difficile de s’orienter et de trouver rapidement les salles ou les œuvres que l’on cherche. Prévoir du temps et un bon sens de l’orientation ! Malgré ça, une visite à faire absolument.
Gratuit pour les - de 26 ans.

1ere fois j'ai trouvé cela très jolie et impressionnant le contraste ville et l'intérieur une fois passée l'entrée on a l'impression d'être ailleurs c'est magnifique très grand jardin allée malheureusement aujourd'hui les bassins étaient vide ce qui diminue le charme

Terrificante. Non si può gestire gli ingressi di un museo del genere in una maniera così approssimativa.
Voto 1 stella ma sarebbero zero.
Consiglio: prendete il biglietto in funzione del fatto che ci sarà almeno un'ora e mezza di coda, se volete entrare per le 11 prendete per esempio il biglietto delle 9.30 e vi faranno tranquillamente entrare senza fare coda

Consiglio di prenotare i biglietti in anticipo dal sito ufficiale perché si fa la coda comunque prenotando(più corta) ma almeno si ha il posto. Lo staff gentilissimo ci ha fatto saltare la fila perché pioveva e mio figlio sul passeggino si stava bagnando. Entrati si scopre che tutto è meraviglioso e le audioguide funzionano davvero bene e sono comodissime per chi ne vuole sapere di piu. L'esposizione però non va in ordine cronologico ma fa dei salti avanti e indietro che alla lunga risultano fastidiosi. Non tutte le stanze sono accessibili con gli ascensori se non portandoti a perdere in dedali di corridoio infiniti alla cui estremità devi chiedere al personale( senza arrivare ad una soluzione perché alcune stanze tra cui la scultura greca non sono visitabili se non facendo le scale!). La mostra è infinita quindi consiglio di focalizzarsi su quanto interessa e scorrere rapidamente il resto altrimenti si fa presto a rimanere tutto il giorno al museo. Per il resto la location è meravigliosa e le opere altrettanto. Per le opere di punta c'è una folla di gente incredibile ma è normale. Alcune ale del museo sono periodicamente chiuse per ristrutturazione, per manutenzione o semplicemente per riallestimento quindi consiglio di informarsi perché non ho trovato prima di prendere i biglietti molta chiarezza a riguardo. Comunque una visita che consiglio

Architektur und Atmosphäre sind grandios. Die Massen der Menschen verteilen sich dann doch ganz gut. Die ausgestellte Kunstrichtungen sind wie immer Geschmacksache. Uns hat die integrierte Haute couture Ausstellung von Kleidern sehr gut gefallen.

J’ai évidemment adoré le Louvre : le lieu est magique et absolument magnifique.
En revanche, il y a toujours énormément de touristes, ce qui rend la visite parfois un peu fatigante, et l’orientation à l’intérieur reste assez compliquée.
Une expérience grandiose malgré tout.
Proposition : réserver une journée ou plusieurs par mois aux citoyens français ;)

Con razón el museo más visitado del mundo, a parte de las grandes obras destacar también la belleza interior y exterior del edificio.
No le doy 5 estrellas ya que a mi parecer deberían de reducir el nºde turistas ya que es un tanto agobiante en bastantes ocasiones.
Por otro lado realice una visita guiada en español que ofrecía el museo (previo pago), decir de ella que la guía fue excelente, pero a penas dio tiempo a la explicación de 4 obras , creo que deberían de alargar el tiempo de las visitas para no quedarte con la sensación de que la visita no merece la pena.
Recomiendo planear la visita, tener en cuenta que son 14 km de obras artísticas.
Alışveriş merkezinin içindeki girişten girmenizi tavsiye ederim. Sıra hızlı ilerliyor. Gezmesi çok keyifli. Önemli eserlerin önü çok kalabalık oluyor.
Beeindruckender Ort. Wir waren aufgrund der langen Schlange nicht drin. Ein Muss f it r jeden Paris Besucher!

(Museum pass in içine dahil) benim en hayal kırıklığına uğradığım yer burasıydı. Harika bi yapı harika bir yer ama aşırı karmaşık. Buraya geliyorsanız 1 gününüzü ayırmanız gerekiyor. Etrafta inanılmaz tablolar heykeller yapılar var fakat herkes mona lisayı görmek için birbirini eziyor. Diğer sanat yapıtlarına bakan yok hele ki üst katlarda çok güzel eserler var fakat oraya nasıl gidileceğini bile çoğu insan bilmiyor. Önerim tüm gününüzü ayırmanız ve aşşağıdaki avm tarafından giriş yapmanız. Müzenin üst kısmında da bir sürü güzel yapıt var fotoğraf çekilmelik.

Non si può visitare Parigi senza fare tappa al Louvre, una tappa obbligata per gli amanti dell'arte e della storia. Il museo è vastissimo, quasi dispersivo, e richiede una buona organizzazione per essere apprezzato al meglio. Uno degli aspetti da migliorare riguarda la gestione dell'afflusso intorno al quadro più famoso, La Gioconda: considerato il numero enorme di visitatori, soprattutto nei periodi di alta stagione, sarebbe opportuno regolamentare meglio l'accesso per rendere l'esperienza più godibile. Il personale si è dimostrato comunque cortese e disponibile.
non ho visitato i musei, parlo dell'edificio e del cortile. Molto bello e colpo d'occhio emozionante

2024/12訪問。
モナリザとミロのヴィーナスを中心に全体を回りました。

モナリザがやはり一番人気で、モナリザの展示フロアから目の前に辿り着くまでに30分ほど時間を要しました。一生に一度は見たいという思いもあり、近くで見ることができて感動しました。

計画的に回れば半日程度あれば充分に見て回ることができるので、予め狙いを定めていくことをオススメします！
Cada esquina del lugar es realmente hermoso. Un lugar que sí o sí debes visitar, y en cuanto a la compra de tickets no hubo problema

Stupendo, peccato non riuscire a vedere la Gioconda se non in mezzo alla folla, dovrebbero studiare qualcosa per defluire meglio il pubblico, le altre ali del museo sono molto snobbate dal grande pubblico, vanno tutti lì nello stesso istante. Con tutto quello che c’è da vedere.
Superbe musée, un incontournable sur Paris. Seul bémols, le monde, l’amabilité du personnel et la propreté qui mérite d’être améliorée.

Ein wirklich unglaublich großes Museum. Interessante Ausstellungsstücke.
Natürlich unglaublich viele Menschen und teilweise sehr unangenehme Luft in den Räumen.
Wir haben uns trotz Karte ein paar mal verlaufen, aber dann auch recht schnell wieder zurechtfinden.
Rechtzeitig da sein ist sehr wichtig! 30 Minuten vor Eintritt war trotz Nebensaison nötig.
Très belle découverte, prévoir le plus tôt possible dans la journée pour profiter au maximum car le musée est gigantesque

Огромен ренесансов дворец и комплекс едно наистина красиво място което впечатлява с колосалните си размери.В цяла Европа има дворци но сравнение с този ще ви се сторят като малки къщи.Днес това е музеен комплекс  в който има експонати от различно естество  като всичко е разделено на сектори.Тук е и апартамента на император Наполеон.Навсякъде има указателни табели за картината Мона Лиза ,но ако искате да видите друго трябва да питате персонала не е добре указано.Има огромни опашки за влизане проблемът е ,че няма достатъчно гишета където е проверката на багажа и за това се получават тези опашки.Има вход и през търговския център където опашката върви малко по бързо.Един ден не е достатъчен за обиколката.

A very, very crowded place, you can't see anything interesting without being stepped on or harassed by selfies. People come here to stand in line for 30-60 minutes to take 100 selfies instead of visiting a museum.

Het Louvre blijft een indrukwekkende plek vol geschiedenis en cultuur. Maar het prachtige historische uitzicht wordt helaas verstoord door die moderne glazen piramide in het midden van de binnenplaats. Het voelt totaal misplaatst en haalt de charme weg van een plek die zoveel historie ademt. Jammer dat zo’n belangrijk erfgoed ontsierd wordt door wat aanvoelt als een commerciële beslissing. Je vraagt je af wie hier vooral beter van is geworden… Zeker geen meerwaarde voor de beleving van het museum zelf.

Some of the staff were very helpful. Others allowed for other tourists to butt in line, which i was not a fan of. You have a line for a reason not to allow a free for all. Otherwise the museum was nice, tickets are good for 2 hours which kind of force you to rush through it, unless I misunderstood the ticketing. Very crowded, but to be expected.

Very pretty inside and out but horribly structured and they closed some exhibits early. Not really sure why that is allowed. They closed Napoleon’s apartments before 6pm and the museum is open until 9pm. Very little explanation on what some of the rooms with gorgeous ceilings were prior to being an exhibition. Would have preferred more of a history on that.

Beautiful, but very poorly managed: it is very difficult to move around the museum to visit all the rooms and sections. Notably, the Gioconda room is extremely packed and smelly, while some sections very close to it are completely empty and ignored by tourists. Also, several lifts were suddenly closed without explanation, and some toilets were very dirty.

Visited the museum with high expectations, but unfortunately, our 11:30 AM booking didn't quite go as planned due to a staff strike. We weren't allowed in until 5 PM. Given the museum's massive size, one day is hardly enough to take it all in. The Mona Lisa, while famous, felt somewhat overhyped.

Tips for future visitors:

- Arrive early to beat the crowds
- Bring drinking water to stay hydrated
- Book tickets in advance to secure your spot
- Be prepared for long lines if you don't get in early

Despite the challenges, I hope my review helps others plan their visit!"

Had a ticket in advance and was able to line up 15 minutes before my time and got inside in 20 minutes. But I saw they let people for earlier session in right away…. So maybe just buy your earliest ticket and show up when you can.

Nice art work and awesome sculpture! For art geeks I recommend to spend a whole day here cuz there’s a lot to see. I did it in 3 hours and there was still section I had to skip because it was overly crowded :( I was there on a weekday. It’s so packed that there were people who got push and tripped over others.

Line for bathroom is long but the cleaning lady guided us to the one for the group section and it was empty.

The guy at the ticket check point is just fed up with all the people who couldn’t scan their QR code , I suggest you crop it out big before entrance.

It’s truly and once in a lifetime opportunity to see so many great paintings, sculptures and many objects, but due to the crowd , I spend most of the time watching out for people than to enjoy the place. Might not go back soon.

The paintings and sculptures are beautiful, but the experience was kinda disappointing. The museum is huge and the map is useless, we walked in circles just to find the Mona Lisa, and when you finally get there you can only take rushed photos before being pushed away because of the poor organization and massive crowd (also some places smell bad).

The descriptions are also often just stating the obvious, and many are only in French. The bathrooms were in terrible condition: overflowing with paper, floors full of used paper, water not draining, sinks dripping, and every woman going in was clearly grossed out. A staff member even came in, saw the mess, and just left.

We also couldn’t see everything we wanted, because even with directions we kept getting lost. I would honestly recommend coming with a guide, otherwise it’s very difficult to navigate and enjoy the visit.

The Louvre Museum is undeniably impressive and full of amazing art, including the famous Mona Lisa. The building itself is stunning, especially the glass pyramid.

However, it was extremely crowded, which made it hard to enjoy the experience fully. The surrounding area also had a strong smell, which took away from the overall vibe.

Still worth a visit if you love art, but go early or booked to klook and manage your expectations.

Amazing place. But it is absurdly crowded. I only went to two wings. The building itself is a marvel. Like elsewhere is Paris and France there is no air conditioning. Compared with the crowds and the lines, it was hard to stay longer and enjoy much. It is truly marvelous but you have to go during non-peak times to take it all in with pleasure. I plan to go from 6-9 on days it is open late in the future. Even the external of the building is incredible in addition to the art and artifacts, so it is a must see. I took off two stars because I was so stressed out there.

Loved and hated this museum. Unbelievable collection of many of the finest art in the world surrounded by mobs of clueless "influencers" all vying to snap themselves with works they fail to appreciate beyond a catchy background.
IDK why they bother with timed entry. The corridors and collection rooms are frequently mobbed to impassable.

A Majestic Experience with Some Practical Hurdles for Families

The Louvre is undeniably breathtaking—an immense facility filled with some of the world’s most stunning and iconic art. It’s a must-visit at least once in a lifetime for any art lover or curious traveler. We pre-booked a tour with the tour guy.

However, for families with small children, especially those using a stroller, the experience can be a bit challenging. The museum isn’t very stroller-friendly. On the day we visited, the elevators weren’t working, and we had to carry our (fairly bulky) jogger stroller up and down stairs—definitely not ideal. If you’re visiting with a baby or toddler, an umbrella stroller would be a much better option.

The museum was very crowded, which made navigating with a stroller even more difficult. We were told there is a baby room, but it was extremely hard to locate. We spent over 30 minutes asking multiple staff members, receiving different directions each time, and eventually gave up. We ended up breastfeeding and changing our baby behind a staircase after getting approval from a kind staff member. Definitely not what you expect from such a world-renowned institution.

Also, while the Louvre is mostly indoors, some rooms can get surprisingly warm on summer days. We were lucky to visit on a cloudy day, but even then, a few sections were uncomfortably hot.

All that said, the art and architecture are absolutely worth experiencing. Just be prepared if you’re visiting with young children—it’s doable, but requires patience and flexibility.

Must visit for first time tourists in Paris BUT dont Fall for the super overpriced Cafés and Restaurants Inside. They Sell mostly supermarket food for Michelin Star Prices. Also you will want to prepare for masses of rude tourists. Dont expect respectful manners!

Pro tip: dont only visit the Louvre to make a few photos of the Mona Lisa. Make sure to Check out the recent Art exhibitions

Museum is so large I dare you to finish it. Whoever designed the museum clearly did not think about the visitor experience. Most of the antiques are stolen from other civilizations. Could’ve been smaller without them. Although accessible, there are a lot of stairs to descend and ascend for walking around the same floor.

This museum is just enormous. Be prepared to walk and walk and walk some more.  We went on a Wednesday evening and it was not very busy. Disappointed that such poor directions and lack of elevators. Literally walked around in a circle looking for Napoleon area. Even with asking for assistance.

Great museum, need more than a week.  Mona Lisa painting is overrated, not worth the lines and effort.  Many other, better works if art.  Bathrooms not so clean.  Lots of stairs, could do better at wheelchair access and directions to elevators.

Most negative reviews tend to come from the wait times. Le Louvre is one of the most famous museums in the world, of course it's going to be busy when you reserve a mid-day time slot.

Regarding the extra wait with reservations: the queues outside of the pyramid entrance are for security (they only have one x-ray scanner and one metal detector for the entire entrance). Everyone, even people without tickets, funnels into the same scanner. Crazy. However, it's still good to reserve in advance; otherwise, you'll be waiting in another queue beyond security for tickets.

My advice: book the earliest time and arrive 30 minutes before opening. Or, arrive later in the afternoon (17H+). I took advantage of their free entry with no ticket (first Friday of every month beginning at 18H) and was able to get past security in about 15 minutes when arriving outside at 17H.

Upon exiting, I also discovered a second entrance that's accessible through the "shopping mall" that you're forced to walk through when exiting the museum.

Aside from the security choke point, my biggest disappointment comes from the poor layout/direction. The paper maps are essentially useless. The main levels are segmented in numerous locations with staircases that either don't allow you to continue on the same level or cover multiple floors with no access to the intermediate levels (eg: a staircase can take you from level -1 to 1 with no way to access 0).

Adding to their layout disaster, multiple exhibits that contain access to other (open) parts of the museum were closed, forcing me to backtrack in order to continue viewing rooms on the same floor.

The art collection at Le Louvre is undoubtedly impressive. You can appreciate almost every piece (aside from the Mona Lisa madhouse) without an overabundance of crowding. Research the collection before arrival so that you can better plan your visit.

Overall, Le Louvre is an impressive museum. Aside from navigating the maze, your experience will be better with proper planning.

We had a timed entry pass but still had to walk from the carousel entrance over to the main entrance for some reason, that was never explained. Then waited for an hour to get in. Once in the museum they had an amazing selection of art and artifacts. But much of the sculpture was blocked off for maintenance. We saw Napoleon’s apparent which was interesting. Obviously this is an incredible museum, but is so popular and large that it takes a lot of patience and time to appreciate. Still worth it if you’ve never been there.

Incredibly busy. Even with tickets bought prior, incredibly LONG line. Slightly confusing. One line was for those with tickets (time frame tickets btw) others were last minute tickets. Felt a bit unfair. A lot of time felt wasted waiting in line. Museum itself was so overcrowded hard to peacefully enjoy the art and get any pictures while inside. But the museum itself interesting, beautiful & endless it feels.

We had tickets booked for Monday at 9am, received an email to say that opening may be delayed but no specific time was mentioned. Arrived at 9 then waited in huge line until 10:30 when still not open then gave up & came back later. No explanation mor apologies were given, terrible PR!
Came back later in the afternoon and got priority entry - great art but signage and directions is somewhat lacking.
Overall the experience was marred by poor management.

The Louvre is an incredible museum, but the experience was ruined by how unbelievably crowded it was. It felt like a zoo inside, with people packed shoulder to shoulder and shuffling through the halls. It was nearly impossible to move around, and at times I had people bumping into me and stepping on my toes. The crowds were simply intolerable, making it hard to appreciate the art or enjoy the visit. If you go, I strongly recommend finding a time when there are far fewer people, otherwise the experience will be frustrating and overwhelming.

Went on Wednesday without buying a ticket in advance. The queue to security moved quite quickly but the line for buying tickets was very long and moving slowly as only 2 counters were open, so in the end we got tickets from the specially able tickets desk. Museum was overcrowded especially in front of the Mona Lisa in the Denon wing. We managed to do the Louvrelite and just wanted to leave as it was really hot and long day. Be prepared for loads of walking and stair climbing.

身障或是無法爬樓梯的一定要現在最外面櫃檯問清楚路線，第一個工作人員跟我們說的路線不對，讓我們在裡面繞不出去，裡面都是樓梯！為了去蒙娜麗莎在裡面繞了兩個小時了🤦‍♀️坐了無數個電梯😮‍💨還沒看到就要先累死了
蒙娜麗莎要坐電梯K才過得去！如果你一開始做錯 後面可能要一直繞各種展廳，到處換電梯才到得了，他們到電梯不是每層都通👎🏼

Za duzo ludzi, a wystarczy ograniczyc liczbe biletow. Niezbyt mila obsluga. Tak naprawde to odwiedzanie Luwru, zeby podziwiac starych mistrzow mija sie z celem. Zeby zaliczyc program obowiazkowy wizyty w Paryzu to i owszem ma sens. Za to ogrody Tuileries wspaniale

En este museo encuentras mucho arte, además de que muchas personas, independientemente sea temporada alta. Hay mucho que ver, recomiendo investigar las obras que quieres ver, no hay sanitarios cerca ya dentro en las exposiciones. Para ver la mona lisa, hay demasiada gente acumulada, apenas y puedes respirar, además de que se ve muy chica, porque la cintas de seguridad están a 5 metros de ella!! Los souvenir dentro del Louvre cuestan un riñón, y eso que ni siquiera son la gran cosa… me decepcionó un poco esta parte

Ho visitato il museo dall'apertura (9 del mattino) fino alle ore 17:00.
A seguito della mia esperienza, credo sia opportuno fare due recensioni distinte del Louvre, uno per la parte dei dipinti Italiani ed una per tutto il resto.

DIPINTI ITALIANI
Immaginate un miscuglio tra uno zoo ed un mercato del pesce.
Seppur quest'ala contenga un patrimonio culturale inestimabile, è anche il punto di ritrovo di chi è lì solo ed unicamente per fare qualche foto "instagrammabile".
Qui troverete soltanto maleducazione ed assenza di consapevolezza situazionale.
Impossibile anche solo sentire i propri pensieri dal casino che c'è, figuriamoci osservare ed apprezzare un dipinto.
Di seguito elenco alcuni punti salienti da me osservati:
- Appena varcati i tornelli, vedrete le persone gareggiare per le scale al fine di potersi aggiudicare un posto in prima fila alla Gioconda.
- Durante la visione dei dipinti mi sono trovato più di una volta a dovermi spostare perché la modella di turno voleva il posto tutto per sé così da poter fare un servizio fotografico con le solite pose stereotipate. Una volta finito il photoshoot, non c'è neanche lo sforzo di dare un'effettiva occhiata al dipinto.
- Alcuni dipinti erano stati rimossi per restauro, lasciando solo la cornice. A quanto pare un muro bianco incorniciato è tutto quello di cui la gente ha bisogno perché erano innumerevoli le persone che lo fotografano.
- Pur essendo vietato mangiare e bere, qualcuno ha ben deciso di ignorare il divieto ed inciampando ha rovesciato una tazza di caffè tra due dipinti che fortunatamente si sono salvati.

Il culmine si raggiunge una volta giunti alla Gioconda. Per arrivarci bisognerà infilarsi in un corridoio pieno di persone che sapranno solo spingere arrogantemente con una mano e fare foto con l'altra.
Nel mentre, il povero personale non farà altro che urlare ai visitatori di non usare selfie stick e flash con scarsi risultati. Una ragazza dietro di me è stata sgridata per aver usato il flash ed ha avuto anche da ridire a riguardo iniziando a litigare.
Una volta arrivati in prima fila, un buon 90% delle persone tirerà fuori il telefono, farà una foto al volo senza neanche guardare il dipinto (i più audaci faranno anche un selfie) e poi se ne andrà.
Se a voi della foto non frega niente, sappiate che non ci sarà modo di godersi l'opera a causa dei continui spintoni di chi vive la vita alla "mordi e fuggi".
Concludo dicendo che a mio parere la Gioconda è ormai in balia del turismo barbaro che l'ha resa sopravvalutata e famosa per tutti i motivi sbagliati, pertanto mi sento di sconsigliarvi la visita.

TUTTO IL RESTO
Tranquillità e silenzio, con opere meno conosciute ma comunque interessanti. Allego le uniche foto da me fatte delle cose che più mi hanno colpito.

IN BREVE:
Se entrate nel Louvre solo per la Gioconda o per fare gli intellettuali sui social, fate un favore a tutti e non entrate.
Se invece avete interess

Expositions temporaires de haut niveau, avec des panneaux explicatifs bien conçus. Dommage que l accès aux toilettes soit si compliqué, le Musée ferme à 18 h mais certaines toilettes sont fermées dès 17 h. De même pour certaines cafétérias fermées dès 17 h ( en période de canicule !!)

Tickets im.voraus gekauft, nebeneingang genommen, trotzdem in einer mega langen Schlange über 30 min auf den Einlass gewartet. Drin war es ok, nur an bestimmten Stellen überlaufen. Aber! Beschilderung der Wege mehr als mangelhaft. Wir waren ständig auf der Suche nach dem richtigen Weg. Mitarbeiter entweder nicht da in der Nähe oder nicht besonders hilfsbereit. An der sicherheitskontrolle wurde ich sau blond angemacht, weil ich dem Mitarbeiter nicht in die auge geschaut gaben und nicht gelächelt habe. Er hat die Kontrolle nur aus diesem Grund angehalten und mich bloß dargestellt. Ich fand es mega unangenehm!!! Alles war sonst in Ordnung, mein Ticket gültig und keiner von uns hat verstanden, was er eigentlich wollte. Eine schlechte Erinnerung habe ich auf jeden Fall.  Ansonsten habe ich ein besseres Konzept erwartet

El Louvre compta amb una col·lecció extraordinària, però l'experiència de visita es veu condicionada per l'elevadíssima afluència de públic. En molts espais resulta difícil aturar-se a contemplar les obres amb comoditat. A més, l'extensió del museu fa que sigui inabastable en una sola jornada i l'organització dels itineraris i la informació disponible poden resultar poc intuïtives. Tot i el seu indubtable valor patrimonial, la visita pot arribar a ser esgotadora i menys satisfactòria del que s'esperaria

Dentro do edifício deveria ter melhores indicações de onde nos encontramos o mapa que existe não é perceptível.
Não conseguimos ver tudo porque estávamos sempre às voltas.

Çok kalabalık, çok karışık, çok karmaşık bir mekan. Temizlik, tuvalet gibi insani boyutlardan bahsetmek dahi istemem. Bunun yanısıra müzenin çevresi de tamamıyla tarihi ve turistik bir alan. Turistlerin/insanların gezme alanlarına toz toprak sermelerinin sebebini anlamadım. Ayakkabınız üstünüz başınız bembeyaz toz içinde kalıyor. Yürüdüğünüz alanda toz bulutları yükseliyor. Alanda adam akıllı bir tane çöp atma kutusu vs yok. Açık alanda tuvalet ihtiyacını giderecek bir alan yok. Taharet musluğu olan bir tuvalet yok. Başka enteresan olay da bu kadar yoğun turist akınına uğrayan alanın araç trafiğine açık olması. Çok ama çok saçma. Hele polislerin art arda siren çalarak turistik tarihi alandan son sürat sürekli olarak geçmesi ayrı bir garabet. Lafın özü mekan güzel işletmecisi beceriksiz. Ayrıca sanat tutkunu biri değilseniz zaman kaybetmeyin derim.

Ortaçağdan kalma haritalar ile yer bulunması oldukça zor. 21. Yüzyıla uygun olarak duvarlara yer yön belirten elektronik harita konulabilir. Bunun haricinde 2. Kat kapalıydı fakat bu bilet alırken belirtilmedi. Gerçi Napolyon'un kıymetli eserlerini çaldıran yönetimden fazlası beklenemezdi. Tüm bunlar harici kesinlikle görülmesi gereken bir kültür mirası.
E’ scontato dire che è bellissimo ma e’ una fila per vedere ogni opera ed è complicato orientarsi.

Lotado demais, sempre, já fui 4 vezes, tem obras maravilhosas mas é muito mal sinalizado. Vejo muita gente perdida   Banheiros fechados, muito pouco funcionando para os milhões de pessoas que visitam por dia. Vários funcionários para controlar a multidão que vai ver a Monalisa mas nenhum em vários lugares que precisaria para dar informações. Eles sabem que estão sempre lotados então não se esforçam para melhorar as condições. Tinham que controlar também o número de entradas assim como faz o Vaticano

Vstup od oblúka je neoznačeny, ťažko ho bolo vidieť a hlavne keď sme prišli z druhej strany. Múzeum je obrovské, je čo chodiť. Je tam toho veľa. Pri obraze Mona Lisy by bolo vhodné urobiť lepší spôsob prístupu návštevníkov, nakoľko väčšina ide hlavne za ním a je tam tlačenica. Urobiť jednoduché odelenu "chodbu" a nie voľný priestor a ten je oddelený od obrazu. Skrátka katastrofálne riešenie, a niektorí návštevníci sú extrémne nedočkaví čo sa tlačia jak * hriešnici do neba.

Die Warteschlange war leider sehr lang. Die Empfehlung liegt bei reservierten Tickets im Voraus. Die Mona Lisa - das Touristenhighlight - ist es definitiv nicht wert. Nur Gedränge und es riecht nach Mensch. Das Gemälde selbst ist viel kleiner entgegengesetzt meiner Vorstellung. Die anderen Ausstellungen sind interresant, auch der Innenhof hat ein nettes Ambiente.

El museo es enorme e impresionante, pero ármate de paciencia que se llena horrible, considero que debería poner limitante de personas por día
Así mismo los baños están súper sucios, al ser un museo caro no entendería porque los baños están tan sucios

C'est évidemment blindé de gens mais c'est très bien organisé et le personnel est très sympathique. Il vaut mieux réserver sa place en ligne avant. Et si vous voulez voir la Joconde, comptez encore une heure d'attente dans la salle..
Musée a visiter sur plusieurs jours tellement c'est IMMENSE !

Compré las entradas del supuesto primer viernes de cada mes " GRATIS" y no se especificaba que no se puede entrar a las exposiciones,sólo a lo que ya se puede ver incluso desde fuera del museo .Hacer cola para que te sigan que no hemos comprado nada y que las exposiciónes se pagan.Deverian especificarlo en la web y no nos hagan hacer el idiota "comprando online"

Beaucoup trop de monde.
Il n'y a pas de sens logique, on s'y perd très facilement..
Cela reste quand même un lieu d'exception.

Muzeum jest gigantyczne!! Mnóstwo eksponatów. Najbardziej podobały mi się eksponaty egipskie i greckie. Minus taki że Mona Lisa jest zasłonięta i żeby zrobić zdjęcie trzeba stać w kolejce, a gdy chcesz stanąć z boku i zrobić zdjęcie by nie stać w tłumie obsługa cię przepędza

Unbedingt Tickets online kaufen, wir mussten schon alleine mit Tickets eine drei viertel Stunde anstehen, ehe wir rein konnten. Meine Herzensempfehlung ist das Abendmal von Da Vinci, da kommt die Mona Lisa nicht gegen an. Wer hier nur für sie Eintritt zahlt, wird enttäuscht. Mir hat besonders gut die Vielzahl an Statuen gefallen (mit unter die Venus de Milo!!!).

Le musée du Louvre est évidemment un lieu magnifique. Rien que l’extérieur vaut le détour : la pyramide, les bâtiments, l’ambiance autour… c’est impressionnant, majestueux, on sent tout le poids de l’Histoire et de la culture française rien qu’en s’en approchant.
Mais malheureusement, je ne peux pas donner mon avis sur l’intérieur parce qu’on n’a pas pu y entrer. En pleine période de vacances scolaires, après avoir réservé un logement à Paris en partie pour ça, on a découvert une fois sur place qu’il était impossible d’acheter des billets directement. Il faut réserver en ligne, à l’avance, et quand on s’y prend le jour même ou la veille : tout est complet.
Je trouve ça vraiment regrettable et incompréhensible. C’est un musée immense, iconique, visité par le monde entier… et pourtant, aucune possibilité de venir spontanément, même en faisant la queue. On n’est pas les seuls à avoir fait le déplacement pour rien ce jour-là. Beaucoup de familles, de touristes comme nous, qui avaient juste envie de découvrir un grand musée… et qui sont repartis frustrés.
C’est dommage, surtout pour un lieu aussi symbolique. L’extérieur est splendide, bien sûr, mais ça laisse quand même un goût amer de ne pas pouvoir vivre cette expérience parce que l’organisation ne prévoit aucune solution pour les visiteurs sur place.

Un musée exceptionnel mais un accueil déplorable. Les agents sont froids, pas du tout accueillants, voir très désagréables. Je comprends qu'il y ai su monde. Mais il y des touristes étrangers pour qui être dans ce musée est un rêve et se voir accueillis de manière aussi désagréable n'est pas professionnel et surtout très décevant. Cela ne donne pas une bonne image de notre pays.

The Louvre, celebrated globally for its exceptional collection of 615,797 objects (with approximately 35,000 artworks on display across eight curatorial departments), unfortunately falls short in terms of public management and queue organization. While the museum's artworks are undeniably priceless and awe-inspiring, the visitor experience is often marred by confusion and frustration stemming from poorly managed entry processes.

Although booking a timed slot in advance may seem like a logical way to ensure smoother access, walk-ins appear to gain entry just as easily, making the system feel inconsistent and arbitrary. The lack of clear communication and visible organization at the entrance is disappointing, especially for an institution of such prestige. It’s surprising that a museum of this stature hasn’t implemented a more efficient and visitor-friendly system.

The challenges don’t stop at the entrance. The museum map is vague and confusing, and the signage within the museum itself does little to help visitors navigate its vast and crowded spaces. This lack of clear guidance detracts from what should be a seamless and enriching experience.

A visit to the Louvre should be about appreciating the creativity and skill behind each masterpiece—not about enduring overcrowding and logistical chaos. The current approach seems more focused on maximizing visitor numbers rather than ensuring their safety and enjoyment. In the event of an emergency, such as a fire, the safety of visitors—often numbering up to 30,000 daily—feels deeply questionable.

Tip 1: Plan your visit carefully in advance to optimize your experience and reduce potential frustrations.
Tip 2: Be aware that some exhibits are reproductions, not original pieces.
Tip 3: Keep in mind that the provenance of certain items in the collection is subject to debate.
Meh.  The outside is the best part.  Inside is just the kind of dull stuff-under-glass museums that have uninspired people for generations.

We didn't like the visit to the Louvre that much, the museum is very confusing. We booked the audio guide but unfortunately it was very difficult to use, even the tech-savvy visitors in our group had difficulty using the device. You never got the information you were looking for, the directions were misleading. In my opinion the audio guide was a waste of money, a good old map would have done better. Unfortunately the Mona Lisa was so crowded that there was only a disorganized jostling and shoving, not for art lovers with claustrophobia.

I have never been to a museum where finding things is so hard as I did in Louvre. The directions are horrible. The maps are terrible. Organization is poor. Even after pre-booking u have to stand in queue for ages. Workers r rude. What a mess
I am not commenting on the artifacts. 99%of its taken from other countries. A small country like France has the largest number of time zones. That proves my point of colonization.
Anyhow, if you wanna visit here, 1 hour in line plus whatever time you wanna spend. It's closed on Tuesdays.

About exhibitions, they moved some, I visited on 2017 and loved the exhibit for "Pandemonium", it was aweosme the lighting and white background was just perfect in the section of Foreign artists. I went to see it today on may 2025, and is just horrible, the lighting is all wrong, on a red background, nothing relatively close to the experience I had on 2017, totally disappointed.

Map was almost not useful to move trough the museum. Access is not well controlled, many line skippers and allowed rude people to push others.
Bathrooms NOT available, we were in floor 1 looking for one, reached the doors and info said it was not available but other in xxx rooms are open, we went there and no, red rope on it denying the access. We ended going outside earlier to find a bathroom in a restaurant nearby.

Felt as more of a tourist attraction than a place for someone who wants to be there immersed in art history. I didn’t get an audio guide since my native language wasn’t available (which I’m not blaming them for, even though it’s a widely spoken language and would benefit a lot of guests, but whatever) (+a long queue) and thought the labels would still explain enough. I didn’t expect that everything would have a long text, but pretty much nothing besides the very famous pieces did.

The instructions on where to go are not that clear, except for Mona Lisa.

There are some places to get food inside, but they’re on the somewhat pricier side.

If you’re sure you want to go, it’s still worth it, but otherwise there are more museums in Paris that might be more worth your time.
Get there as early as possible and be prepared for the whole place to be HUGE. I was disappointed in the way it’s organised but will still come back

Maps were impossible to figure out. Lines take long time and many people just pushed in. Toilets were filthy and poor female cleaner had to clean mens toilets while they were still in there. Females have to line up for bathroom. I got there early and was charged 3 euro in shopping gallery to use bathroom. Paintings were beautiful but MonaLisa was fenced of about 50 metres with huge crowd you couldn't even see it. Much walking on creaky floors and many many stairs. I went three times and saw everything but was hard work. Egyptian and Easter Island area was closed which they didn't let you know. Some paintings were hung really high so I couldn't see very well. I'd give the art 5 stars, was amazing, but the Louvre was a maze of filthy toilets,confusing hallways and overcrowding.

Unfortunately, The Louvre does not live up to its reputation. It barely qualifies as an art museum. It is mostly "object art." It is thronged with tourists who are trying to take photos of literally everything (Why???). It has reduced art to pure commerce. Just look at the slop in the gift shop. There is no soul or integrity.

My review is not about the masterpieces that the museum hosts. These are beyond any criticism.. I’m reviewing the modern people who run the museum: the museums paper map is almost useless! Extremely difficult to read, because it’s totally different from the reality. Also, renting the audio guides it’s super difficult to understand what you have to do. It could be more efficient and easy for people. And perhaps they could limit the number of people visiting the museum. It was super crowded.

Beautiful museum. So much artwork to see outside of the Mona Lisa. This museum is large and requires hours on end for multiple days to truly enjoy what the Louvre has to offer. It was very crowded. If you don’t like crowds, I would suggest a different activity. It felt like there were thousands of people inside.  The artwork covers a variety of cultures and spans a millennia. Note when leaving the museum the French police/ train security are running a scam. The station at the louvre requires you to buy a train card for €2 and you will still have to buy a train ride for €2.5 euro. You cannot buy 2 one way tickets for the same train card. They will end up charging €50. If possible avoid the Lourve train station when leaving. I am still floored they would try something like in an alleged developed nation.

* For sure it’s one of the best museum in the world and recommended to anyone to visit at least one time, but unfortunately it’s not organized well, directions and maps are not enough to lead you. There are few staff and they are not supportive!!!
* The crowd may hurt you, some people stick to some subject or pictures and there is no rules to prevent. maybe it’s better to limit the capacity or organized this masterpiece better to improve the quality of visitors.
* In the other hand there are lots of closed parts. If you are insist to see any special part, before going there check the closed part. I really got disappointed with this because I reach there after a long journey and my favorite part was under constructio😒
* the building itself is very fantastic, the object and pictures are wonderful but I don’t enjoy my visit.
* buy online ticket in advance.
* snack and sandwiches are available but their prices are more than normal.
*Internet is not available, I download the head out application before going there but it doesn’t work there, so I couldn’t use my online explanation and I waste my money, it’s better to buy headphones and online explanation from somewhere else not head out.

One of the worst experiences in a museum in France. No clear indication where to go and what path to follow in order to see everything it has to offer. They know it's big so they count on that to make you come again and see what you missed. You start on a floor, in order to do that whole floor at a time you go down and up other floors more than once so you can come back to it. No clear path to follow, people going in all directions instead of following at least some basic "lanes". Besides this, you get to stay in line each time you want to enter a wing through the center pyramid. They should split it into 3 wings, with separate tickets, that way people who want the Mona Lisa would get only that ticket and others can enjoy sculptures and the rest of the wings peacefully. Each wing would follow a path and return you to the center pyramid where you enter the next wing. Much more organized and clear that way. I had to go through places i had already seen more than 3 times to get to somewhere new because orientation is a nightmare in this museum. Luckily I won't be coming again anytime soon. Unless they make it free.

Great collection otherwise.

When you arrive you need to make sure you get into the correct line. There were 2 when we arrived. One said 11.30 (it was 11.55 when we arrived) the other was all times on the hour.
The person on the 11.30 line couldn't / wouldn't tell us what the line was for.
The other staff on duty also couldn't tell us what that line was for.
I noticed a lot of people lining up in that line only to be told they needed to go to the other line.
I would advise booking your ticket though as the booked ticket line did move pretty quick despite it looking really long. Whereas the none booked ticket line hardly moved at all.
Once inside the gallery is really hard to navigate. It is a really busy place once you get inside people moving all over.
When I arrived there were more lines inside but no signage to say what they were for.
The information desk gave away maps which helped the navigation issue but the staff manning the desk were unhelpful and 2 we tried to talk to wouldn't look up from their phones.
We didn't visit all the exhibits as it just didn't do it for us.
We like reading about what we are looking at and occasionally like asking for more information however, when walking round we noticed so many employees asleep all we could think of was Mr Bean in Paris.

All in all a bad experience happy to have see it but wouldn't go back.

Muzeum ma wspaniałe zbiory, tego nie sposób negować. Sam budynek też jest imponujący. Natomiast organizacja zwiedzania to porażka. Mieliśmy bilety kupione online na 11,00 godzinę. O 10,30 wchodziła jeszcze grupa z biletami na 9,00. My weszliśmy z opóźnieniem 45 minut. Organizacja przed wejściem to jakaś prowizorka. Najpierw trzeba było stać i czekać na pustym placu, a potem przed wejściem ochrona popędzała ludzi jak stado owiec. Muzeum nie jest przystosowane do tak dużej liczby odwiedzających. Nie ma szatni. Toalety albo są nieczynne, albo są do nich długie kolejki. Brakuje miejsca by usiąść i odpocząć. W niektórych miejscach tłok jest taki że ludzie prawie się tratują.

I've bought the tickets in advance with a stated hour of entrance, but when I arrived there was an hour long queue for people with tickets. So what's the point of stating the time on the ticket? It was a weekday, I can hardly imagine what's happening at the weekend.
Many paintings are with Franch description only, no English.
In Islamic gallery there were items without any readable notes as the text was demaged.
The number of people inside is outrageous. It's impossible to see anything as everyone is pushing you and it's so noisy that I bet everyone is exiting the museum with a headache.
The audio guide is a Nintendo console, it helped a lot, when I wanted to find a particular piece but at some points it was disappointing too. It includes information not about all the pieces of art, but it covers only about 30%. Sometimes there was just a general description of the room with no info about paintings. And be ready that in 3,5 hours the battery will die and you'll have to go back to the reception. If you are on the 2 level of the far wing it'll take you 15 min one way.

The crowds! Really uncomfortable and unpleasant. Couldn’t enjoy any of the art as it was mobbed. It would still have been busy if they halved the crowd. I normally enjoy museums, but this was a most unpleasant experience. The venue is spectacular, I’m sure the art is also but it was just not possible to enjoy it.

If I had the same experience most people reviewing seem to have, I think this would be a lot higher. Unfortunately, the day I visited a lot of the museum was closed (which I did not know about when buying the tickets), and areas continued to close unexpectedly as we made our way through the museum. Because of all these closures, it made navigating extremely difficult and confusing. I left feeling like this was the worst museum I have ever been to. Glad we still got see the Mona Lisa though.

Upon reaching out with my concerns, I was informed that they update the room closures weekly. So don't buy your ticket two months in advance like I did! Though even some rooms that were listed as being open on the website were indeed not, so idk.

one of the worst experienses of a 6 day trip on paris....I really don t ubderstand the good comments....everything that doesn t come from ancient greece or from ancient egypt is mediocre and indeferent....also the labels of many artifacts don t have description in english!!! how am I supposed to know what it is or where it comes from? lastly the museum its self is chaotic and not in a funny manner....the maps that they give you have diferent markers and check points than the ones on the halls of the museum!!! you can t recognise where you are in the museum based on the map of the miseum that they have created!!! there doesn t exis even one simple "you are here" sign....all that and the high ticket prices makes it a bad museum experience...

Classic art museum but whether the costs and hassles are worth it is debatable and it's really become a tourist trap. Tickets must be purchased in advance and most have been bought up by tours...as such, you have no flexibility when and how you visit and the staff are neither helpful nor friendly. I hate to give this a poor rating, but such is Paris now. And why did it close on Tuesday??

There was no wait, because we arrived early (11:30) , but after us at 12:00 the line was like 200 people to enter, even with tickets. Many many people. The map is just horrible and overall confusing. We went upstairs because we wanted to see Anne of Cleves portrait, since it’s advertised everywhere, and it’s on of Henry VIII wives. We went there, and it was shut, and no one even said why. We asked one of the workers and he just said “come back tomorrow, MAYBE it will be open”. Come on dude! If you’re in Paris, go to Musee de Orsee instead. Heaps better.

First of all, bad organized for entry ( with tickets reserved and paid ) and too bad audio Manuel guide and expensive !!

Not very well led for visiting insides, then for the Mona Lisa ~ it is disaster messy ~

The price is not worth to go in and out for a messy short time !

Disappointed!!

The security at the entrance of the pyramid was quite moody. She refused to let me enter through the pyramid entrance and instructed me to use the carousel entrance instead. I had a booked ticket and arrived on time, while others were allowed to pass through. The ticket clearly states that I can use either entrance. I was there to appreciate art, and beginning my visit with such an arbitrary and unfriendly security person was not an ideal start.

An absolutely must visit, but they need to do something about the amount of people getting in at the same time. Your visit can be ruined by how crowded it is inside. You can barely move in the crowd, nether the less enjoy the art.

Hey Louvre Management, you guys really need to get a freaking grip! Seriously there is no way people can appreciate these amazing works of art when it is nearly impossible to see them! Way too many people visiting at once, even with pre booked tickets we had to queue several times. Audio guide is a total waste of money when multiple guided tours happen all at the same time - at one point I had three guides speaking at once right next to me - insane!
Toilets are filthy- if you let that many people in, you need to get more staff members to manage the load! And of course why would anyone had any regard for visitors forking quite a substantial amount of money for a ticket and ask the protesters out???
Visiting the Louvre was the main point of our trip to Paris and it left us bitterly disappointed.

Way too busy to enjoy. Came to see the Mona Lisa. Bought tickets online for time slot, still had to wait in line outside. The Mona Lisa room was absolutely packed and you cannot get close at all, the closest they let you get is like 5 meters away and the painting is pretty small. You can take your selfie but forget about enjoying the actual painting.

This was the second time I'd been to the Louvre, the first being in 2016. And while it's awesome to see the difference in the Louvre this isn't in a good sense. The collections feel overwhelmingly large and laid out in strange ways, and the signs offer little to no help. It feels like you can walk around in circles and get nowhere.

It's not to say that there isn't amazing pieces of art here of course there is. However be prepared to wait for hours and then experience more chaos on the inside... Buy tickets to avoid the queues above ground but still be prepared for a wait.

An absolutely fascinating museum only if you speak French!
Most descriptions are only in French, and the audio guides offer very limited language options, making it difficult for international visitors to fully engage with the exhibits.
There is a large section dedicated to Iran, yet there is no Persian option in the audio guides, and not even English translations on the signs below the objects. As an Iranian, it’s heartbreaking to see your country’s treasures displayed abroad without enough effort to present them in a more inclusive and responsible way.

Great to visit, but as with the palace of Versailles, it’s way too over commercialised, the timed ticket that we bought weeks ago means nothing as the queues are massive, not enjoyable as they let too many people in at a time, and it’s full of ‘influencers’ asking you to move so they can get a good photo or video.. the Mona Lisa room was a joke, you couldn’t get near to see, they should limit the numbers allowed in

Nice place, but if you miss to take the audio guide - you are doomed :) 95% of the explanation texts for the artifacts are in French only.
Several staff members were quite rude without a reason when they started pushing us (me and the kid) to leave the museum at 17:20 even though the exit was only 5 minutes away.
I’m aware that they have to deal with various audience all the time, but that’s not the way to handle customers.

Overall - poor experience for me personally.

Just come early. Wear good shoes. Be patient with everyone.
Expect half of the world to visit exactly at the same time as you and the other half to arrive an hour later.

Take away the Mona Lisa and the crowds would cut in half.

Bigger crowd than in the subway, everywhere, a disaster...
Pass... Walk through la cour carrée, avoid the pyramid, check the arc de triomphe du carrousel, and go to le musée du jeu de paume....
Mode enjoyable...

Richileu wing was largely closed as was several other rooms such as Room 652 where I went to Louvre specifically to see. Rooms are not clearly numbered which makes following their map impossible. Very few museum employees to ask for help and at least one who was gave in correct advice. Spent 2.5 hours wandering and mostly lost trying to find specific items I cam to see....very dusappointing
The outside was beautiful. After waiting several hours to get in on a reserved tour time we were told to leave because museum security went on strike.  I bet it must be wonderful to see.
Way over crowded hard to see anything.  A waste of my time and money. Will not go back.

Crazy crowded. Miles of darkened paintings needing restoration. Hardly anywhere to sit and rest/enjoy/sketch the art.

Most annoyingly, after you are worn out you are forced to exit through a long shopping mall. Squeezing every dollar from visitors.

If you go, for heaven's sake, study some of the other da Vincis besides the Mona Lisa.

Too crowded and expensive.  Few impressionistic paintings. We were ocean deep to see Mona Lisa. Go to Musee d' Orsy instead for the impressionists and Musee d'la Orangerie for the 360 large Monet's water lilies.
I've been to many galleries, this is not my favorite. Once they move the Mona Lisa out it may be tolerable.  My sympathies with the staff.

Super crowded. Paid for and booked tickets in advance, although that didn’t speed anything up. General admission without tickets was speeding along beside us, not sure what the point of buying tickets online and choosing a time slot was. There was another queue inside the museum just after security to enter any of the exhibits inside the museum. The art and history in the museum was spectacular, although most areas were beyond crowded, and overwhelming.

We travel to Paris from the US specifically to visit this place. We purchased tickets in advance from their website. The day before our entry, they send an email canceling our tickets due to “exceptional circumstances “. Museum opening was delayed until after 11:15. Have a back up plan. You can’t trust the people who run this place.

It was too hot and there were too many people, so it was difficult to properly view the artwork. In particular, the room with the Mona Lisa was so crowded that it was difficult to move. I couldn't even appreciate the painting. The Louvre needs to control the number of visitors.

It’s a maze. Great works of Art though. Some measures should be taken to better guide the flow of tourists through the building and the different themed rooms. Must book tickets in advance but the ticketing system was f…. So we weren’t able to book for our disabled mother.

The museum delayed opening by 2 hours without explanation. We booked 9am and entered around 11am. Those who booked later time, say 11am, were clueless about what to do. The staff were equally clueless and just told them to come back later. But couldn’t tell them when to come back. Chaotic.

My review is about the organization of the museum, not the artworks. Despite having a pre-booked ticket for a specific time slot I waited for almost 2 hours partly outside in the rain to get inside. All supposedly as the museum opened late because of a staff meeting (?!). Adding to this waiting for half an hour to get into women’s WC. And this was on a weekday.

A lot to see but the organization is terrible, on a level of a small rural museum with no experience. That paper maps are extremely small and now every room is showed, they are grouped together. The rooms on the map have numbers but when you enter a room, there is no number. Some maps are north oriented, other ones are south oriented, very confusing. The labels with description are on a hight of like 80 cm, is it for children or what? It was very uncomfortable to read it. Anyway only couple of them are in English, the most of them are French only.
Some parts of nice exhibitions are closed. They said it was due to insufficient stuff. It's pity I didn't know it before buying the ticket.

In spite of phenomenal collection place is a damn zoo!
And that's in March, God help you in high season. Timed stamped tickets will not prevent you from spending significant time in line

The exposition itself is exquisite, but the lack of respect for the visitors is something I never seen anywhere else. It was puring rain and they forced people to stand outside for about 1 h in a queue, despite everyone, including us, booked entry on a given hour. After entering we quickly realized that there is plenty of room inside to let people wait sheltered from the elements. To add insult to injury the girl responsible for information barely spoke English, guards were running around shouting orders to the visitors in a stressed out, panicked manner. It looked like it was their first day dealing with the crowd. All because there are no signs, no clear information where to go. I feel like I should have refused to stay in the rain and demand a refund. This kind of disrespect encourages more from the museum, knowing that they can get away almost anything, having a world famous exhibition. I repeat, they have a vast underground area with a literal shopping mall in there. A security check queue under a roof was discovered by airports a long time ago. This is not acceptable. I will struggle to recommend Louvre after what I've seen.

One of the world's famous museums with €22 entry ticket on a Saturday is marred with poor upkeep. One of the dirtiest museums that i have visited. Busts are dusty. Floors are dirty as if they are cleaned only once a month. The most crowded part of the museum is where the painting of Monalisa is kept. You can only see her from several feet away. The artefacts displayed are really good but the upkeep of the museum is very disappointing. I will give it another chance in future but this visit was a total disappointment. The 2 stars are for the art and artefacts on display. Q

It is very crowded. I think direction, explainations are not enough. Even toilets and rest areas are not enough. Museum can be visit with tourist guide. Also porte de lions gate is fastest way to enter.

GONG SHOW.
can't even get near it.
The building itself from the outside is amazing. And huge. But couldn't even get close enough to see the front door. I don't mind waiting in line for a bit to get into see something cool, or even pay money to. But I am not going to waste my entire day just waiting in line to maybe get in and still have to pay. Google search and you can see what's inside without waiting hours in line..

My entire life I've heard of the Louvre.  Have always wanted to go.  Took my first trip overseas this last week.  Bought timed entry for 11am only to stand in line for 1.5 hours. When we finally got to the entry area there was only 1 bag check machine and 1 metal detector. How can a museum of this size and stature only have 1 on top of the timed entry meaning nothing?  I know the Mona Lisa is super popular but I've been in mosh pits at concerts with better behavior.  I was squished, sweaty, had people cut in front of me and an all around crap show of an experience.  I will never visit again as the process, set up, staff and all things involved were horrible.

There's alot of people and really tourists are all queuing up to see the Mona Lisa.

I reckon you either go super early, as soon as it's open or very late as after 9am it's flooded with people and hard to navigate your way around the mesume.

This place feels like an international big market, packed with people. Even though we had bought our tickets online in advance, we still had to wait in line for thirty minutes. The staff kept gesturing for us to hurry up. Once inside, it was almost impossible to calmly appreciate the museum's collection because of the large crowds. There wasn’t enough air conditioning, and the air quality was poor. Even getting a drink of water required standing in a long line, as there were no water fountains available. Some of the paintings have reflections from the ceiling or emergency exit lights visible while you’re viewing them.
Overall, it’s just too popular. I hope they can improve the environment and the entry process to allow for a better experience of the art.
Very difficult to find the exits. The good thing is that there are plenty of chairs to let people sitting down.
2nd time visiting, worst than before.. too crowded, they sell much more tickets than should be possible for a day, it makes the visit unpleasant.

I recently visited the Louvre, having booked my ticket in advance, yet was greeted by an exceptionally long queue. Navigating inside felt like walking through a maze, and it took me nearly 30 minutes to find the exhibits I wanted to see.

The good thing was the staff. They were nice and could speak English well. But paying EUR 17 to get in didn't feel worth it to me, especially when there are other museums you can visit for free. The Louvre has great art, but it was too crowded and confusing. I think it's okay, but not the best. I give it 2 out of 5 stars.

The Louvre was below my expectations. I have visited many museums across Europe and there were others that impressed me much more. Inside, everything feels quite confusing and you get very tired walking around. The directions and signage are not very clear. It was also quite warm and stuffy inside. We even struggled to find the exit. Overall, it was not a very enjoyable experience for me.

Museum viewing was brilliant.
But as we had someone in a wheelchair IT WAS A NIGHTMARE. layout way to confusing. Level 0 then (0) then -0. What.
Lifts did go to next floor went to 2 floor above or below. Constantly on the phone asking the rest off the family group which floor they were on. Reply don't know.
Really beautiful but extremely crowded. It was impossible to move around or actually enjoy the art.

Visited the Louvre recently, and while it’s undeniably an iconic museum, the experience was a mixed bag. Yes, I did get to see the Mona Lisa, but only through the sea of phone screens held up by tourists who seemed more interested in selfies than the art itself. The Italian and French art sections were packed and chaotic, making it hard to fully appreciate the masterpieces.

The museum is incredibly crowded and noisy overall, so it’s not exactly a serene cultural escape. That said, it’s still worth visiting for the sheer volume and variety of art on display.

Just a tip: make sure you book a slotted ticket well in advance—aim for the earliest time possible. Otherwise, you’ll find yourself queuing for hours at multiple points. Go early, be patient, and you’ll still find moments of awe amidst the madness.

My goal today was to see one specific artwork. I had researched its location ahead of time, I triple checked that it wasn’t affected by rolling gallery closures. I’m a traveller from Australia, so it’s really important I got this right, as I can’t simply come back.

I arrived at the section of gallery it’s housed in, to find it closed.

There’s a sign and a QR code to scan for more information on closures, which only takes me to a page that tells me this closed gallery should, in fact, be open.

I walk to the other end of the circuit to see if I can access that section of the gallery from the other side. It’s also closed. A guard tells me they don’t know why.

I have a little cry, because at this point I’m frustrated and tired. I’ve come from *literally the other side of the planet*, I’ve done all the right things, what’s going on? I decide to navigate to the nearest cafe for a cold drink to calm down.

The cafe is, you guessed it, closed!

I head to I information to ask about the painting/closed galleries - the man there doesn’t know why the gallery isn’t open, there’s nothing in his notes about it. He’s apologetic and tries to be helpful, and advises that I call ahead next visit (who knows when that will be, home is a 23 hour flight away).

The art is beautiful, but the overall experience is beyond disappointing. Closed wings, broken toilets with long lines, confusing layouts, insufficient places to get water to drink - it’s not exactly what I expected from the world’s biggest museum.

Do better.

I was rather disappointed. After dreaming of going to the Louvre for a long time, not only to see the Mona Lisa, it was a letdown. I was especially looking forward to the Egyptian, Roman and Greek section. However the presentation seemed rather antiquated and could use a refreshing touch. What was a bigger letdown was the fact that almost all the exhibits in the Louvre had only French texts underneath it, not even the second most spoken language... English.
As a tourist wanting to learn something there about ancient relics, statues and paintings was rather lost in translation.
We would have much appreciated it if non-native speakers would have been considered more, since the entry fee of 17 Euro per person ist already quite high.

For a world renowned museum, the staff was unnecessarily rude. You’d think they’d be more than helpful and polite, but instead they were condescending, and will not hesitate to call you names (including “idiot” and “dumb”) in multiple languages, to your face. (They talk badly about customers in front of them if they think you only speak 1 language) Double check your ticket, make sure it’s for the correct time and date, their mobile app allows you to purchase timed tickets for times that have already passed, and will NOT switch your ticket or give any refunds over their faulty system, no matter how polite you are. Also, don’t waste your money on the guided audio tour. It only works half the time, and will have you walking in circles trying to find the checkpoint.

I have visited Louvre museums for 4 time. I really love this museum and I appreciate the art work but what made me give 2 stars for this museum will be “the peak season” visit. It was just horrible and not organized. There were massive crowd to see the Mona Lisa painting (they did place it to different room) but they couldn’t control the massive crowd at all! There were people who came to see the world famous pairing for their perfect selfies and some came to appreciate the painting. But you only get to see the painting up close for 2 min... not to mention so rude people would “pretend” to go to other halls but they just cut the long lines and the staff members doesn’t do anything about it. I think they should provide different tickets with time slot to control the crowd as it took me more than 1 hour to get the temporary hall to see Mona Lisa and according to the staff it will be placed there til October (known for official off-season and that’s when some of the famous places given free entrance as well!).

I definitely not recommend people who loves to observe the painting and appreciate the details to visit on the peak season. I visited during March and October which was more pleasant.
Summer was my first nightmare visit (for those people who can’t stand heat or crowds are definitely not recommended at all!)

Of course I won’t stop other people from visiting wonderful museum as they got different sections and wonderful art pieces besides famous Mona Lisa!

I went with my family and I had booked for the wrong day and wanted a refund as we couldn't as the day we wanted to go was fully booked. They did not allow a refund and when we got there I managed to change the timing however the timings for my family had all changed to different times so we had to buy another set of tickets.
Absolutely not worth the wait and the staff was extremely rude and the Mona Lisa isn't worth all the hype.
Would I go again? No
Do I recommend it? No

I've studied art history since I was 16, and have been waiting to see so many art pieces. I planned a tour of the art pieces long before I left. When I got there I was able to see less than half the ones I planned on seeing, including the one I want to see most! (David's, Oath of the Horatii).

This was because many sections are closed in different days. That is so dumb and annoying. I was pissed, every other painting I asked about, the workers there said, that it's closed. Closed, closed, closed! Unbelievable. Only on Sat & Sundays everything is open.

Also, there was a painting I saw, and when I wanted to go back to it and asked where it was, many of the oblivious workers said, it was too closed, and weren't convinced by the fact that I saw it, and of course later I found it. Annoying. I'll remind you that unlike many other amazing museums are free by donation, and here you have to pay.

The directions and map of the place is terribly designed, embarrassing really, confusing, inconsistent and not practical. The numbers of the rooms are not placed the same in every room for example. There's no use of color coding, a big hot French mess. The designers should be ashamed of their work.

I think the worst part is how the coordinator decided to organize the art, I couldn't find a logical progression, nor is it explained anywhere.

The biggest problem is, that all these genius and timeless paintings are held captive in the beautiful madhouse, and we are held hostage to come and suffer while trying to enjoy these masterpieces.

So very disappointed. Everytime I got to see a painting or sculpture, I was momentarily blissful, until have to deal with trying to see the next one. If I could choose to see them in a different place, I would. But, se la vie...

The Louvre is iconic, but unfortunately, it was a disappointment. It is far too massive to navigate easily; we spent more time lost in wrong hallways and stairs than looking at art. The crowds around the Mona Lisa make it impossible to enjoy. Out of all the museums we visited in Paris, this was our least favorite experience

When you don't have a culture.. You project a foreign culture.. This is the only truth.. the museum is very nice, but a large part of the history that hides inside it is not his.. my friends, history is not for sale!

SOOO overrated. VERY crowded. Extremely Overpriced. Incredibly stupid audio guide that didn’t work and was extremely confusing. I love Nintendo so I was excited when the audio guide was a Nintendo DS. But I ended up being so disappointed when the software that shows you around on it was stupid and gimmicky. And this is what annoyed me the most about the Louvre - you can only go into one of the three wings of the museum ONCE! What’s the point of that?! You pay for a ticket and then can’t make the most of the place! And annoyingly, the signs of the place are all wrong! I followed the signs to the Mona Lisa and they were wrong... oh well lol and yeah, there’s a lot of art which is impressive but the museum needs to take a leaf out of the British Museums book and fix up its design and audio guide.

The most famous museum in the world (?)...
You can see everything, but why there aren't information labels in English in every exhibit? Plan your visit early on the morning, visit the most popular spots first and then manage accordingly the time you want to spend

Well, I was hoping this would be the best museum I experienced in Paris but alas, it was not. Despite having ticket,I stood in the timed entrance line for 90 min. Once inside,it was jammed w folks taking numerous photos & selfies, blocking walkways & bumping into slower folks. Restrooms where hard to find,no toilet paper & dirty. Trying to find a way to get to other floors if a person is handicapped, was difficult. I did see the Mona Lisa from a distance & watched as others held cell phones over folks heads to get a picture. There was security there ti stop folks from taking a photo from the side of the exhibit.  I enjoyed the Impressionist museum so much more!

Mijn bezoek aan het Louvre viel helaas erg tegen. Allereerst was het personeel moeilijk te benaderen omdat veel medewerkers geen Engels spreken, wat het stellen van vragen of het krijgen van hulp erg lastig maakt voor internationale bezoekers.

Daarnaast werkte de website slecht tijdens het kopen van tickets. Ik kreeg meerdere foutmeldingen, waardoor het proces onnodig frustrerend en tijdrovend werd. Voor zo’n wereldberoemd museum verwacht je een beter werkend online systeem.

We have been wanting to take the boys to the Louvre to show them the great creations & works of art. We made it. The obligatory Mona Lisa by da Vinci.
Ac word of advice - if your want to avoid the mass of people, pks go to the Islamic Art & Hellolinistic pottery sections + Workd Class Museum = great food. 👍🏽👍🏽

Слишком много людей на вход и внутри. Никакого удовольствия от посещения. Конечно, само здание как внутри, так и снаружи впечатляет. Как и мысли о хранившихся внутри сокровищах. Но спокойно рассмотреть их не удалось. Кажется, что альбомы с репродукциями картин смотреть удобнее и приятнее.

I wanted to love it, but... way too many people are allowed in at one time and while we had timed tickets for 11:30 we stood in the rain for 1/2 hour before we managed to get inside. The area by the Mona Lisa is ridiculous - everyone crammed in one section like cattle, taking selfies and allowed to spend so much time taking pictures. Why not have a revolving floor to move people along? Why not have people in a single-file line? Why have a massive clump of people? Sigh.
Also, there are too few bathrooms and my goodness the smell was absolutely foul in the 3 that I used throughout the day (women's room) that reeked of stale/old urine. Completely disgusting.
It would be much better to increase the price of tickets slightly and then allow fewer people per day. There were just too many people that you couldn't move at times. Additionally, when we were in the main, center part at the entrance, I asked the gentleman at the information desk if there were other cafes around the museum or if they were all just centrally located right there by the entrance? He said they were all right there. So we waited and finally got a meal there. After touring around the museum more, we found TWO cafes that were located out in the wings. Unbelievable.
Lastly, when we tried to leave after 5 hours of touring around, we could only find our way out through the underground mall area. Horrible signage and it was incredibly difficulty to get out.

Louvre at this stage should be free. It's not really worth the ticket. There are many rooms closed.

The staff is always in high nervous alert given to a possible terrorist attack.
You are a world famous museum with world famous artworks and not such small prices, yet you somehow manage to have horrible toilets and it seem like you take no care of them at all. Fix it!
Very warm inside the museum, you have to queue quite a while for a good view/picture of the mona Lisa painting.

Far too crowded and badly organised. Long queues for toilets and restaurants inside.
Have been there in the past when it was much quieter.
Despite having prebooked, we still stood in line for an hour inside the shopping area.

Very frustrating experience as a wheelchair user. We came across three broken lifts, which meant that out of three wings and three floors on each, we could only get into two wings and only one floor in one and two floors in another.
